runners.name: Opportunity
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; good fourth in Listed race at Goodwood last spring; gelded and off 11 months, shaped promisingly when third of 14 on handicap debut at Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago, doing his best work late on; nudged up 1lb but still looks seriously unexposed; strong claims.
runners.comment: Promising third on handicap debut at Ascot, best work late on; unexposed; strong claims

runners.name: Topteam
runners.spotlight: Four wins from 11 starts; successful on comeback at Kempton (1m4f, AW; made all) in April and again ran well when second at Newmarket (1m4f, good) two weeks ago; should be in the thick of things once more.
runners.comment: Four wins from 11 starts; good second at Newmarket last time; in the mix once more

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Tough 6yo with seven wins to his name; beaten favourite at Hamilton (1m3f, good) last time but shaped quite nicely, keeping on into second place after a slow start and meeting trouble; place possibilities again at the very least.
runners.comment: Didn't get rub of the green when second at Hamilton; place possibilities again at least

runners.name: Tiernan
runners.spotlight: Close third of seven in Listed event at Hamilton (1m3f, good; 150-1) last summer, but that run stands out like a sore thumb from the rest of his form and he was well beaten (after wind surgery) over the same C&D on reappearance; too much to prove for comfort.
runners.comment: One run stands out from the rest of his form; well beaten on reappearance; enough to prove

runners.name: Ghaiyya
runners.spotlight: Likeable front-running filly; made a winning return at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) and typically gave her all when second at Hamilton (1m5f, good) three weeks later; edging up the weights but set for another bold show.
runners.comment: Likeable front-runner; followed Doncaster win with Hamilton second; should go well again

runners.name: Per Contra
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb lower at Newcastle last autumn but hasn't repeated the form and slow starts are becoming a habit; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle last autumn but hasn't repeated the form; slow starts becoming a habit

runners.name: Real Dream
runners.spotlight: Fourth in handicap in Saudi Arabia on second start this year but has not beaten a rival in two runs back in Britain; difficult to recommend.
runners.comment: Has not beaten a rival in two runs since returning to Britain; difficult to recommend

runners.name: Rogue Millions
runners.spotlight: Highly impressive C&D winner (soft ground; handles good) on handicap debut last June; didn't build on that in two subsequent runs but has been gelded and switched stables; still low-mileage and his new yard has been among the winners; fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Impressive over C&D on handicap debut; didn't build on it but still low mileage; new yard

runners.name: Enemy
runners.spotlight: Durable veteran who won twice on the AW early this year; however, he was soundly beaten at Newcastle on Good Friday and faces some unexposed rivals on his return to turf.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Newcastle on Good Friday and faces some unexposed rivals on return to turf

runners.name: Claymore
runners.spotlight: Campaigned in the UAE this winter, last seen finishing tailed off at Meydan; still 4lb higher than for his last win in Britain and probably best watched after three months off.
runners.comment: Last seen finishing tailed off at Meydan; probably best watched after three months off

runners.name: Dancing In Paris
runners.spotlight: A third runner for Ian Williams; ran some fine races in defeat last season, including at Glorious Goodwood (1m6f, good to soft); returns after nine months off over a trip probably short of his optimum.
runners.comment: Tough 6yo; returns after nine months off over a trip probably short of his optimum

runners.name: Star Harbour
runners.spotlight: Irish-trained 8yo, on a long losing sequence; not entirely disgraced when fourth of ten at Leopardstown (1m4f, good) two weeks ago; others have more potential but he's certainly well treated on his best form.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence but not entirely disgraced when fourth at Leopardstown last time

runners.name: Castle Stuart
runners.spotlight: Respectable sixth over 1m2f both starts this year, the latter in a warm race at York when shaping as if worth another try over 1m3f+; gets that here and has each-way hopes.
runners.comment: Respectable sixth both starts this year; should be suited by this trip; each-way hopes

runners.name: Wild Clary
runners.spotlight: Found only Realign too strong at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last autumn despite meeting trouble at a crucial stage, and he was back on the up when a neck second of 16 at Ripon (6f, good to soft) six weeks ago; reliable and has notably solid credentials.
runners.comment: Back on the up when beaten neck at Ripon; reliable and has notably solid credentials

runners.name: Recency Bias
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo with a progressive profile; beaten neck when odds-on at Newcastle (7f) last month; usually travels strongly and should be at least as effective back at 6f; still on a workable mark and high on the list.
runners.comment: Unexposed and progressive; travelled strongly when beaten neck last time; high on the list

runners.name: Station X
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who posted a career best when seeing off five rivals at Southwell (6f) in April; beaten only narrowly when sixth in blanket finish at Hamilton (5f, good) last time; reliable, and should be in the mix again.
runners.comment: Front-runner; followed Southwell win with close sixth at Hamilton; in the mix again

runners.name: Grant Wood
runners.spotlight: Almost as good as ever at the age of seven and went down narrowly at Ripon (6f, good) earlier this month; hard to knock but this is a warm race and he faces some unexposed rivals.
runners.comment: Almost as good as ever but this is a warm race and he faces some unexposed rivals

runners.name: Realign
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo with something of an all-or-nothing profile; put everything together when bolting up at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last autumn; might have needed the run when only ninth of 13 at Newmarket on reappearance; leading claims if building on that and cheekpieces may give him a lift.
runners.comment: Bolted up at Haydock last autumn; might have needed reappearance; strongly considered

runners.name: Native Honey
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come over 7f at Ayr, and he surged back to form when scoring there ten days ago; still lightly raced, in excellent hands, and should be at least as effective over this trip on a stiff track; a danger to all at the foot of the weights.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; surged back to form when winning at Ayr; dangerous off bottom weight

runners.name: Mister Sox
runners.spotlight: His six successes include four last season; retains all his ability but he's still above his last winning mark and he was only eighth at York last Saturday.
runners.comment: Retains ability but still above last winning mark and only eighth at York last Saturday

runners.name: Havana Rum
runners.spotlight: Went close at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) on reappearance but had no obvious excuses at Doncaster next time; more exposed than some of these and 6lb above his highest winning mark.
runners.comment: More exposed than some of these and 6lb above his highest winning mark

runners.name: Gressington
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts since December AW success, finishing in midfield at York (40-1) on return to turf last time; beginning to look well handicapped but needs a revival.
runners.comment: Out of sorts since December win; beginning to look well handicapped but needs a revival
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Brosay
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back 6f wins at Windsor (good to firm) and Ascot (good to soft) in 2025; has taken a while to get going this season but he shaped as if coming to the boil when sixth of 14 at Newbury (led briefly over 1f out) two weeks ago; back to his last winning mark and should be at concert pitch now.
runners.comment: Shaped as if coming to the boil when sixth at Newbury; should be at concert pitch now

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: Had a solid 2025, winning three times (5f/6f), but hasn't finished in the first eight this year; dropping in the weights but needs to turn a corner; perhaps a change of headgear will help.
runners.comment: Hasn't finished in first eight this year; needs to be revived by a change of headgear

runners.name: Rousing Encore
runners.spotlight: Promising second on reappearance at Newcastle and better than the bare results suggest since, meeting significant trouble in running in a big field at York last time; could play a major role if getting the breaks.
runners.comment: Met significant trouble at York last time; could play a major role if getting the breaks

runners.name: Marty Hopkirk
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in last four handicaps but there was more encouragement in his fourth to Redorange (runs in the Listed race at 2.33) at Windsor 12 days ago; has raced mainly at 5f but should stay further; not without hope.
runners.comment: More encouragement in fourth at Windsor 12 days ago; not without hope

runners.name: Fivethousandtoone
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, only 1-27 on grass; never the slightest threat on return to turf at Leicester; others have more convincing profiles.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner, only 1-27 on grass; others have more convincing profiles

runners.name: Jordan Electrics
runners.spotlight: Spent last year in the grip of the handicapper after seven wins in 2024; more leniently treated now and ran respectably when sixth of 28 in the 7f Victoria Cup at Ascot; seemed unsuited by drop back to 5f next time; this intermediate trip is probably ideal these days and he commands respect.
runners.comment: Best recent efforts at 7f; seemed unsuited by 5f latest; this intermediate trip is ideal
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Redorange
runners.spotlight: Classy sprinter who built on an eye-catching reappearance when justifying strong support in handicap at Windsor (5f, good) 12 days ago; found only one too good in French Listed event last summer and looks a strong contender back in this grade.
runners.comment: Justified strong support in handicap at Windsor 12 days ago; big chance back in this grade

runners.name: Luna A Inbhir Nis
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; most progressive last season, making it six wins for the campaign in handicap at York (5.4f, good) in July;  probably needed the run when down the field there on reappearance; stiffer task up in grade.
runners.comment: Won six times last season; probably needed run on reappearance; stiffer task up in grade

runners.name: Starlust
runners.spotlight: Top-class form when third in Group 1 Nunthorpe at York and when winning Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint at Del Mar, both in 2024; rusty on last year's reappearance but showed he retains plenty of ability when fourth of 20 in Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot on only subsequent outing; leading claims if showing his best after layoff, although that's not guaranteed.
runners.comment: The one to beat if running to best after layoff, but was rusty on last year's reappearance

runners.name: Celandine
runners.spotlight: 0-6 since winning the Group 2 Lowther (6f, good to firm) as a 2yo, although she had an excuse (awkward leaving stalls, never a threat) in 6f Listed event at Newmarket (13-2) on reappearance; below par on two previous attempts at 5f, but not ruled out if on a going day for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Had excuse on reappearance; not ruled out if on a going day for in-form yard

runners.name: Rogue Lightning
runners.spotlight: Globetrotting gelding whose last win came in Qatar (6f, good) in February 2025 when Washington Heights was second; has run respectably on his two outings this season, fourth in French Group 3 (5f, soft) on latest; should be thereabouts under optimum conditions.
runners.comment: Globetrotting gelding; respectable fourth both outings this season; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Ten Pounds
runners.spotlight: 5-10 at 6f/7f for Harry Charlton; unplaced all four outings since joining current stable, finishing in midfield in Victoria Cup at Ascot most recently; unproven at 5f; wears headgear for the first time; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Unplaced all four outings since joining current stable and is unproven at 5f

runners.name: Washington Heights
runners.spotlight: Close third in Group 2 Temple Stakes at Haydock 12 months ago and made most to win Listed race at York (5f, good to firm) last summer; well below par all four starts since (short of room in Newmarket Group 3 on latest) but down in grade here and may well bounce back.
runners.comment: York Listed winner last summer; short of room last time and may well bounce back

runners.name: Azure Angel
runners.spotlight: 6yo mare who sprang a 22-1 surprise in Listed race at Bath (5f, good) in April, swooping fast and late; likeable type with an impressive strike-rate but she'll need another personal best under a penalty.
runners.comment: Sprang 22-1 surprise in Listed race at Bath; will need another personal best under penalty

races.race_name: Betway Lester Piggott Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly Known As The Pinnacle Stakes)

runners.name: Estrange
runners.spotlight: Very talented 5yo whose two wins last year included this race (1m4f, good; at Haydock) on reappearance; ran a cracker when second in Group 1 at Ascot (1m4f, good) on final start; that makes her the clear form pick back in this grade and she's the one to beat if ready to roll after 224 days off.
runners.comment: Won this race last year; second in Group 1 final start; the clear form pick after layoff

runners.name: Azaniya
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last season, her four wins from just five outings culminating in a clearcut Listed success at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, heavy; also handles good to firm); shaped as if needing the run when fifth in Group 3 there on reappearance; gives the impression she'll relish this extra yardage, and with further improvement on the cards she's high on the list.
runners.comment: Progressive; Listed winner last season; probably needed reappearance; high on the list

runners.name: Crepe Suzette
runners.spotlight: Ended her 3yo campaign with an excellent third in Doncaster's Group 2 Park Hill Stakes (14.5f, good to soft); bit disappointing when only fifth in Listed race at Goodwood (11-8 favourite; beaten over 7l by Coedana) on return; now on a retrieval mission in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Fine third in Group 2 last term but disappointing on reappearance; now wears headgear

runners.name: Naga
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick of handicap wins at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) in April and did well in the circumstances when a never-nearer third at Ascot last month; rises steeply in grade here but still has untapped potential.
runners.comment: Followed three handicap wins with unlucky Ascot third; up steeply in grade but unexposed

runners.name: Coedana
runners.spotlight: Improving with virtually every run, and was even better than the bare result when second in Listed race at Goodwood (1m4f, good; came from a long way back) four weeks ago; up in grade again but still has more in the tank and not to be underestimated.
runners.comment: Upwardly mobile; fine second in Goodwood Listed race last time; not to be underestimated
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Patagonia Girl
runners.spotlight: Has made incremental progress over 11 starts, overcoming a wide draw when beating 14 rivals in handicap at Chester (1m4f, good) three weeks ago; may be open to further improvement but that win came off a mark of just 80 and this is a significantly stiffer assignment.
runners.comment: Keeps getting better but Chester win came off mark of just 80; much stiffer task here

runners.name: Waardah
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of Azaniya; seemed to relish the step up to 1m6f when winning the Group 2 Lillie Langtry at Goodwood (good to soft) last summer; didn't give her running in Group 1 at Ascot on final 3yo start, finishing well behind Estrange; may still have untapped potential but this trip looks a bare minimum.
runners.comment: 1m6f seemed to suit when she won Goodwood Group 2; this trip looks bare minimum on return

runners.name: Sharpen
runners.spotlight: Sole win in Ireland came in 1m2f maiden but she was also a good second in a 1m4f Listed event; changed hands for 360,000gns in December; plenty of improvement required on British debut but she has switched to a flourishing stable and is not without hope.
runners.comment: Best effort in Ireland was a Listed second; lots to find but new yard is flourishing

runners.name: Princling
runners.spotlight: Runner-up last October on both 2yo starts (7f, AW/good), on the second occasion in a novice at Newmarket that has worked out very well; reappeared with a ready win at 1-4 in Wetherby novice (1m, good to firm) and there's every chance that this unexposed colt will be better than today's opening mark.
runners.comment: Two seconds and a win in novice events; probably better than today's opening mark

runners.name: Inishbeg
runners.spotlight: Fourth attempt when winning a Thirsk maiden (7f, good) in April; made most when below form on handicap debut at York (7f, good) 17 days ago, which can probably be forgiven from widest draw and he was second favourite that day; 5lb claimer is recruited for this hot race on first crack at about 1m.
runners.comment: Made most when below form on handicap debut at York (7f, good) but was drawn widest
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Blue Courvoisier
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with close second on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m, good) last month, followed by a respectable fourth of 11 at Ascot (1m, good; favourite); has had only four races but probably needs to resume improvement.
runners.comment: Only four races (2nd and 4th in 2026 handicaps) but probably needs to resume improvement

runners.name: Lake Como
runners.spotlight: Found serious improvement over 7f/1m on AW but, although running respectably, he's not transferred the best of that form to 1m turf handicaps on last two outings (good to firm/good); this stiffer track may suit.
runners.comment: Peak AW form was not transferred to 1m turf handicaps last two starts; this track may help

runners.name: Crest Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Made the running when beaten a neck on nursery debut at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last September, which was the second leg of a four-timer for the winner; reappeared with an easy 10l win at 2-5 in a maiden at Southwell (1m, AW; made all) in March and looks well treated today, given that his mark is unaltered since that win.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck on nursery debut and runaway AW maiden winner on return; looks well treated

runners.name: Langstone
runners.spotlight: Gelded after debut and has left sole that 2yo run (6f, soft) well behind him by winning novice events at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) and Kempton (7f, AW) this spring; did well to score with strong finish last time, having been carried left; promising and probably has an advantageous mark for this handicap debut, especially now upped in trip.
runners.comment: 2-2 this term in novice events; promising and probably has an advantageous opening mark

runners.name: Laureate Crown
runners.spotlight: Won on good to firm on debut; gelded before taking a small-field handicap at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in March and he improved again to follow up at Sandown (1m, good) five weeks ago; launched late bids from the back for those 2026 wins, travelled well last time and could have more left in the tank.
runners.comment: Launched late bids from the back for two 1m wins this term; travelled well last time

runners.name: Delinquent
runners.spotlight: Won 1m handicaps at Southwell (AW) in April and Hamilton (good; 6lb higher today) 15 days ago; that's two wins from eight starts and he should be a lot more exposed than most of today's rivals, but he was firmly in charge in first-time cheekpieces on that latest outing.
runners.comment: Should be a lot more exposed than most, but firmly in charge in cheekpieces latest outing

runners.name: Astrazar
runners.spotlight: Won Ffos Las maiden (7.3f, heavy) last September on seventh start, when going beyond 6f for first time, and he followed up in last-gasp fashion in valuable EBF Final at York (7f, good); denied a clear run when mid-division in handicap at Newbury (7f, good; 12-1) on reappearance and the step up to nearly 1m looks a very good move; one to note.
runners.comment: Denied a clear run on return and the step up in trip looks a very good move; one to note

runners.name: Archer Royal
runners.spotlight: Won debut (7f, AW) and came third in Group 3 at Newmarket (7f, good) last September; however, he was last of seven in another Group 3 there (1m, good to firm) on final 2yo outing and beaten 10l in a conditions stakes at Kempton (1m, AW) on March reappearance; the latter was a hot race but today's first-time headgear for handicap debut needs to prompt a vastly better show.
runners.comment: Well held last two starts; first headgear for handicap debut needs to prompt vastly better

runners.name: Sir Albert
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in nurseries last July (7f, good/heavy) and close Listed fifth at Lingfield (7f, AW) this February; has top weight in this hot handicap but 7lb claimer is recruited and the step up to nearly 1m may help.
runners.comment: Close Listed 5th at Lingfield (7f, AW) in February; 7lb claimer is recruited; 1m may help

runners.name: Suspended Sentence
runners.spotlight: Progressive once upped to 1m as 2yo, winning twice (good to firm/soft) before his Listed fourth in France (soft); may have needed last month's comeback run in the race won by Laureate Crown at Sandown (1m, good to firm; 14-1 from 20-1) but he's not obviously well treated.
runners.comment: May have needed last month's comeback behind Laureate Crown but not obviously well treated
runners.owner: G C Myddelton, C F E Hill & Partner

runners.name: Daydreama
runners.spotlight: Won two novice events last summer; easily best of his six efforts since have both been from well off the pace over 7f on good ground at York, including his second in 14-runner race 17 days ago; back up 2lb but has to be worth a go beyond 7f (dam 2m winner) and needs a second look.
runners.comment: Second of 14 from well off pace at York (7f) 17 days ago; has to be worth a go beyond 7f

runners.name: Special Dividend
runners.spotlight: Won last summer on first two starts (6f, AW) then kept on for third of 18 in nursery at York (7f, good to firm) that worked out nicely; well beaten in Newmarket Group 3 (7f, good) on final 2yo start, which was followed by wind and gelding operations this March; also now has a first-time tongue-tie; needs to resume improvement in a major way.
runners.comment: Well beaten in Group 3 final 2yo start; wind and gelding ops this March; first tongue-tie

runners.name: Sponsor
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied when showing significant ability on all three starts, on last two occasions taking minor honours in novice events at Windsor (1m, good to firm) and Newbury (7f, good); needs better on this handicap debut but these are early days and his stable has had recent 3yo winners.
runners.comment: Significant ability; makes handicap debut and his stable has had recent 3yo winners
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Miller And Day

runners.name: Synners Kid
runners.spotlight: Won nursery debut for Joseph O'Brien at Naas (1m, yielding to soft) last September and easily followed up at Bellewstown (1m, soft), both in a tongue-tie; having shot up the weights he reappeared with sixth of ten when 66-1 for stable debut at Ascot (7f, good) three weeks ago, which is his highest rating; could build on that (as he needs to) with tongue-tie reapplied this time.
runners.comment: Dual Irish 1m winner as 2yo; needs to better recent British debut but tongue-tie returns

runners.name: Albaydaa
runners.spotlight: Classy pedigree; ready winner when upped to 7f (AW) on final 2yo start; pleasing comeback at Newmarket (6f) in April, when a keeping-on third of 13, just ahead of a subsequent Listed winner; unfortunate to run up against a big improver at Ascot (7f, good) since but aims lower here; solid.
runners.comment: Pleasing comeback over 6f; ran up against a big improver at Ascot (7f) latest; solid

runners.name: Lady Kodiac
runners.spotlight: Made most for two 6f novice wins on Chelmsford AW as 2yo; respectable rather than dynamic efforts as 3yo, including in a 6f Newmarket handicap last time behind a wide-margin winner who had no pretensions to being way ahead of her mark; needs improvement for step up to 7f.
runners.comment: Two 6f AW wins as 2yo; respectable efforts this year but must improve for new trip

runners.name: Applesandpears
runners.spotlight: Raced clear in 7f and 6f events on the AW last autumn but she was slightly hampered and done with from a fair way out on her handicap debut at Haydock (1m, good); should come on for the run, though, and no surprise if she shows to better advantage in a less competitive race.
runners.comment: Won final two juvenile starts on AW; well held in turf handicap on return; can do better

runners.name: Distant Shore
runners.spotlight: Hooded both starts; never raced smoothly from the word go on Salisbury debut (6f) so did well to force a dead-heat on the line; looked more professional when third under a penalty at Thirsk (7f; also good to firm) four weeks ago and this stiffer test at the trip can suit; should improve.
runners.comment: Very green when forcing dead-heat on debut (6f); creditable 3rd over 7f since; h'cap debut

runners.name: Just A Girl
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner of a Lingfield novice (about 7f, good) last August but she's been on much the same mark, with little reward, since handicapping and a recent second at Newmarket was a remote one; needs weight off back.
runners.comment: High in weights since handicapping and recent 2nd at Newmarket was a remote one

runners.name: Orchid
runners.spotlight: Both wins at 6f on the AW, either side of a winter break as a 2yo and 3yo; seemingly had stamina limitations back over 7f in the autumn and, while she should come on for her recent reappearance at Newmarket, this is a stiff track on which to prove her staying power.
runners.comment: Both wins at 6f on the AW; seemingly had stamina limitations over 7f so far

runners.name: Ziggy's Queen
runners.spotlight: Two narrow AW wins at 6f and 7f over the winter months; never involved back on turf at Thirsk (7f, good) recently and although this is less competitive her stamina for a tough 7f like this still has to be proved.
runners.comment: Two narrow AW wins at 6f and 7f over the winter; never sighted back on turf latest

runners.name: Fouette
runners.spotlight: Back from absence with two 7f AW wins last autumn, then set fire to her mark by running well in Listed events; highly tried on first two starts in 2026 before running quite promisingly at Goodwood (7f, good; first turf handicap) last week, considering she never had a clear run; can fare better.
runners.comment: Won two 7f AW events last autumn; often highly tried since; hampered on turf latest

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Had a wonderful 2025, winning seven times at 6f-1m on a range of surfaces; not much to celebrate this year, and this is her first course run, but back on her last winning mark and a revival should come at some stage.
runners.comment: Won seven in 2025, at 6f-1m; not much to celebrate this year but on latest winning mark

runners.name: St Anton
runners.spotlight: Has proved reliable and progressive in his short career to date; got up near finish when winning at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last month; that looks solid form and he may not have reached his ceiling just yet.
runners.comment: Reliable and progressive; form of Newmarket win looks solid; might be the answer

runners.name: Factual
runners.spotlight: Finished well when fourth of 12 on handicap debut at Chester (extended 7f, good) three weeks ago; remains open to improvement and this stiffer track should be in his favour; leading contender.
runners.comment: Finished well when fourth of 12 on handicap debut at Chester; leading contender

runners.name: Secret History
runners.spotlight: Placed all four starts and she has won her last two; further improvement when coming from the rear to beat seven rivals at Wetherby (7f, good; hooded) five weeks ago; should stay today's extra yardage and there's almost certainly still better still to come.
runners.comment: On a hat-trick after coming from rear to win at Wetherby; open to further improvement

runners.name: Blue To Blue
runners.spotlight: Won three times for Karl Burke as a 2yo and ran a cracker when second at Haydock (1m, good) three weeks ago; hard to knock but he has had more goes than most of these and he was nudged up 2lb after Haydock.
runners.comment: Fine second at Haydock but nudged up 2lb and has had more goes than most of these

runners.name: Horu Kanu
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and can look a difficult ride; rallied into third at Hamilton (1m, good) two weeks ago; needs to be sweetened up by cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-6 and can look a difficult ride; needs to be sweetened up by cheekpieces

runners.name: Wild Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in four races since tackling handicaps, his mark rising from 56 to 84; again had plenty in hand when beating 11 rivals at Windsor (1m, good to firm); impossible to rule out another step forward.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in four handicaps; impossible to rule out another step forward

runners.name: Kanishka
runners.spotlight: In frame all three runs this year, although he didn't go with much fluency when fourth of six at Musselburgh (1m; wore cheekpieces) on latest; more required against some unexposed rivals.
runners.comment: Lacked fluency when fourth at Musselburgh; more required against some unexposed rivals
runners.owner: C Booth, P Boyle, S Coleby & S Smith

runners.name: Sovereign View
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive; won Newcastle nursery last autumn and went down narrowly in Southwell handicap on reappearance; both those runs over 1m on AW (ran respectably on only turf outing); may be ready for a step up in distance but should still be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive; good second at Southwell on reappearance; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Priapos
runners.spotlight: Has done well over 1m for new connections, finding plenty for two wins on sand at Newcastle in late 2025; given a break after and two turf runs since, at Beverley and when fourth at Ayr ten days ago, suggest he isn't just an AW pony; can go well.
runners.comment: Two AW wins at 1m in late 2025; promising on turf since back from break; can go well

runners.name: Count Palatine
runners.spotlight: Useful effort in defeat at Beverley (about 7f) last May and confirmed that stiff tracks suit when winning here (7f, good to firm; sole course start) in August; improved again when successful at Ayr later and no surprise if he comes on plenty from first two runs in 2026.
runners.comment: Won sole course start (7f); improved as 4yo but well held in two runs this year

runners.name: Mr King
runners.spotlight: Modest 2-26 strike-rate and refused to race once in the autumn; however, he's often run well in competitive races, as when third of 19 at York (1m, good) last July and was hampered next time; creditable return for new yard at Redcar (first-time hood, cheekpieces now refitted) this month; involved.
runners.comment: Modest 2-26 strike-rate but has solid 1m form; creditable return for new yard; involved

runners.name: Jez Bomb
runners.spotlight: Four wins in 2025 and he wasn't far away over 1m1f here in September; in reasonable form this year and this trip will suit better than the 1m4f he ran over at York last time out but it will be a good effort if he can win this off his current mark.
runners.comment: Not far away over 1m1f here in September; modest latest; shade high in weights

runners.name: Eupator
runners.spotlight: Two front-running wins on Wolverhampton AW (about 1m and 1m1f) early in 2026; no impact behind Candonomore back on turf at Bath last time, when regular blinkers were left off, as today; likely other days.
runners.comment: Two front-running wins on AW this year; well held back on turf latest; other days
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing I and Partner

runners.name: Marry The Night
runners.spotlight: Three 1m wins on Southwell AW this year; has also won on turf but he's 15lb higher than when last seen on grass in the autumn so needs to show his form on this surface can keep pace with the AW improvement.
runners.comment: Three 1m AW wins in 2026; needs to carry improvement on sand back to turf

runners.name: Moon Beginnings
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; sole win came over 7f at Chester last September and he's shown only the odd glimmer of promise in handicaps on AW and turf since; falling quickly in the weights but cheekpieces have a big job to do.
runners.comment: Winning debut over 7f last autumn; disappointing in handicaps since; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Nbr30, Mike Owen & Rasio Cymru Racing

runners.name: Elizabeth Bay
runners.spotlight: Finished close up in 7f events on the AW for previous trainer but two handicap runs either side of a break were disappointing and her stamina will be under pressure back on this track.
runners.comment: Close up in 7f AW events last summer; disappointing either side of a break; stamina query

runners.name: Ayr Poet
runners.spotlight: All 13 wins have come elsewhere but he does have course form and he did so well when going close at Ayr (1m1f, good to firm) last week; however, he's now 6lb higher than his September win which might be a step too far.
runners.comment: Has course form; excellent second at Ayr last week; now 6lb higher than latest win
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Candonomore
runners.spotlight: A ready winner at Bath (1m, firm) in April and, having run well over 1m2f in the past, he didn't have  a problem with 1m1f when a close third, just behind Ayr Poet, at Ayr since on yard debut; remains on a career-high mark though so a shade more may be needed.
runners.comment: Ready Bath win (1m) in April; similar form when 3rd for new yard latest; career-high mark

runners.name: Freddie Steady Go
runners.spotlight: Cosy winner of minor bumper (good) on debut; had wind surgery after a moderate hurdle debut and, following a couple of fair Flat runs, he caught his rivals on the hop when making all over 7f here (soft) recently; has more to prove off his opening mark but should have some potential.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; two minor Flat runs then made all over 7f here latest; has some potential
runners.owner: Castle View, A Bell, S Oliver & M Severs

races.race_name: Chester Roman Day Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Town Queen
runners.spotlight: Blue Point filly who has shaped well when runner-up in 6f Pontefract novice and 7f Thirsk maiden this spring, leading for a long way on latter occasion four weeks ago; she's open to further progress and rates a big player.
runners.comment: Shaped well when runner-up both runs, leading long way at Thirsk latest; a big player

runners.name: Lucky Luna
runners.spotlight: 6-1, shaped well when a debut fourth of 12 in maiden at Salisbury (6f, good) a week ago; this Lucky Vega filly can take a step forward now, especially over this longer trip; very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Shaped well when debut fourth at Salisbury; can take a step forward over this longer trip

runners.name: Machadadorp
runners.spotlight: 20-1, faded late on when sixth of seven in novice at Ascot (1m, good to firm) on her debut last month; this daughter of Mehmas can build on it here for her leading yard.
runners.comment: Sixth at Ascot on her debut last month; this daughter of Mehmas can build on it here

runners.name: Night Shining
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward from her debut when fourth of six in 7f Yarmouth novice last month; this daughter of Dark Angel can do better still so enters calculations.
runners.comment: Improved when fourth at Yarmouth latest; she can do better still so enters calculations

runners.name: Imelda
runners.spotlight: Fair form shown when placed in a pair of 7f Wolverhampton maidens this spring, third of 11 on latter occasion; this Invincible Spirit filly may do better still but has a wide draw.
runners.comment: Placed in two 7f Wolverhampton maidens this spring; may do better still but drawn wide

runners.name: Navona Grey
runners.spotlight: 25,000 guineas yearling, 22,000 euros 2yo; half-sister to bumper and 1m3f AW winner Galaxy Star (RPR 90); dam, 11.6f-2m winner (RPR 75), is a half-sister to Australian 1m1f Listed winner Greatwood, out of close relative of Gold Cup winner Fame And Glory; the market can guide for this Havana Grey filly.
runners.comment: The market can guide for this Havana Grey filly on her first run

runners.name: Elemma
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Starman; stepped up on her debut when fifth of eight in 6f Ripon novice 13 days ago; handily drawn but more is required here.
runners.comment: Stepped up on debut with fifth at Ripon 13 days ago; handily drawn but more is required

runners.name: Lc Tiffen
runners.spotlight: Only poor form shown thus far, beating one on her first go in handicaps at Wolverhampton (7f) last month.
runners.comment: Only poor form shown thus far, beating one on her first go in handicaps at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Tommie's Angel
runners.spotlight: Poor form shown in a pair of 6f maidens at Southwell and Pontefract this spring; lots more is required.
runners.comment: Poor form shown in a pair of 6f maidens at Southwell and Pontefract this spring

runners.name: Golden Mabel
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in her four runs to date, last of seven on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f) 12 days ago.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much in her four runs, last of seven on her h'cap debut at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Danrana
runners.spotlight: Dandy Man filly who failed to build on her debut fourth when only eighth of 13 in 7.5f maiden at Beverley in August; needs to hit the ground running on her return.
runners.comment: Eighth in maiden at Beverley in August; needs to hit the ground running on her return

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pure Grit
runners.spotlight: 230,000euros yearling by Pinatubo; 8-1, made most when second of ten in Newbury maiden (6f, good) 15 days ago; lots to like if he adapts to this track.
runners.comment: 230,000euros yearling by Pinatubo; 8-1, made most when second of ten at Newbury (6f, good)

runners.name: Caturra Lights
runners.spotlight: Close third in maidens at Musselburgh (5f, good) and Chester (5.5f, good) are both among the best form in this field; a leading contender.
runners.comment: Close 3rd at Musselburgh (5f, good) and Chester (5.5f, good); among the best form in this

runners.name: This Moment
runners.spotlight: Late foal, with useful pedigree for this sort of test; second favourite for six-runner novice at Hamilton (6f, good) 13 days ago but slowly away and a detached last to past halfway, before challenging hard from over 1f out and beaten a head; that's probably among the best form in this and he could be a lot wiser this time.
runners.comment: Pipped at Hamilton; probably among the best form and he could be a lot wiser this time
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Blake's Monarch
runners.spotlight: 10-1, short-headed in 11-runner novice at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) 19 days ago, leading until final strides; while that was a promising debut and he seemingly has the pace for another bold show from low draw here, others probably bring better form.
runners.comment: 10-1 at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) when leading until final strides; not the best form though
runners.owner: The Good Racing Co Ltd & Partners

runners.name: Trust Havana
runners.spotlight: 85,000euros foal, £17,000 breeze-up 2yo by Havana Grey; first foal; dam placed in Slovakia, half-sister to winners Preferential (1m4f Listed) and Rule Of Nature (6f 2yo), out of maiden half-sister to 7f Group 1 winner Etoile Montante; trainer is better known for jumpers but had a 2yo winner here (her fourth race) last term.
runners.comment: £17,000 breeze-up 2yo by Havana Grey; yard had 2yo winner here (her fourth race) last term

runners.name: Deputy Vice
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros foal, 90,000gns yearling, £145,000 breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; Mehmas half-brother to winners Habrdi (6f-1m including 2yo; RPR 92) and Inside Story (6f 2yo; 80); dam once-raced (43) sister to useful 1m/9.4f winner Turnberry Isle, out of Cheveley Park winner Rosdhu Queen; trainer has had two 2yos run this season and one is 3-3, including a Lily Agnes win at this track.
runners.comment: £145,000 breeze-up 2yo by Mehmas; one of yard's 2yos is 3-3, including in Lily Agnes here

runners.name: Dream On Kent
runners.spotlight: By Massaat; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Amber Blossom (7f AW 2yo/7.4f turf; RPR 72) and Not A Sinner (US 6f turf): dam 6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 105), half-sister to Italian 1m1f 2yo Group 3 winner; late foal but with trainer whose latest 2yos have already made their mark.
runners.comment: By Massaat; late foal but with trainer whose latest 2yos have already made their mark

runners.name: Virtuous Star
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns breeze-up 2yo by Starman; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Majestic Myles (7f Listed), Parbold (Group-placed 6f 2yo) and Majestic Moon (useful 6f/7f); stable won this with a newcomer two years ago and this is one to note.
runners.comment: 240,000gns breeze-up 2yo by Starman; stable won this with a newcomer two years ago

runners.name: Koodini
runners.spotlight: Suitably bred and nicely drawn for 5.5f maiden here (good) three weeks ago but rather slowly away and hampered twice in the closing stages when he'd got in touch; weak 13-2 that day, while two stablemates (9-4 and 10-1) finished first and second; capable of better but this time has a wide draw.
runners.comment: Lots went wrong when 13-2 for 5.5f maiden here (good) three weeks ago; now drawn wide

runners.name: Moriarty Moon
runners.spotlight: Has a pretty useful pedigree but last of six in novice at Goodwood (5f, good; 14-1) four weeks ago was a low-key debut.
runners.comment: Has a pretty useful pedigree but made a low-key debut at Goodwood (5f, good; 14-1)

runners.name: Star People
runners.spotlight: Faded to finish towards rear in novice at York (6f, good) 16 days ago but was 8-1, so much better was presumably expected of him.
runners.comment: Faded to finish towards rear in novice at York (6f, good) 16 days ago but was sent off 8-1

runners.name: Metamouse
runners.spotlight: 33-1 for maiden at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) nine days ago when an always-prominent fourth of 12; should improve but has a wide draw and probably some tough company.
runners.comment: 33-1 at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) nine days ago when an always-prominent fourth of 12

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Untried at 6f but he has displayed clear promise at shorter trips, finishing a clear second behind a stablemate at Musselburgh nine days ago (5f, good to soft); fared okay with the draw and Oisin Murphy is booked; chance.
runners.comment: Good second in a 5f maiden nine days ago; up in trip; Oisin Murphy booked; should go well

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: Promise in all three starts, including here, and he ranks highly in calculations judged on RPRs; 6f should be fine but slower than good would be a first and stall 10 tempers enthusiasm too.
runners.comment: Leading form claims now dropping into a seller; draw not ideal but can't discount

runners.name: Everatease
runners.spotlight: He has improved his RPR with each of his three runs in 5f maidens and the last two times he has looked as though this longer trip would suit; slower ground to contend with but more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Improved RPR with each of his three starts; looks ready for 6f; more appealing than many

runners.name: Delete Delete
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; by Massaat; sixth foal; brother to winners Mas Rapido (6f including 2yo/US; RPR 106), Rapido Girl (7f; 74) and Priestess (6f/7f 2yo including AW/Italy; 60); dam placed 5f (including 2yo/AW; 61), half-sister to 5.7f 2yo winner/UAE Oaks runner-up Mary Fildes; stable have had a couple of 2yo newcomers go close; betting to guide.
runners.comment: 20,000gns brother to three winners; stable have had a couple of 2yos go close on debut
runners.owner: Team G Racing & Alfa Site Services Ltd

runners.name: Shaniko
runners.spotlight: By Hello Youmzain; first foal of an unraced dam, herself closely related to French 1m4f winner Imperious Man; hooded for debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Only minor appeal on paper and likely best watched on debut; wears a hood

runners.name: Explainingislosing
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in two 5f maidens this month, latterly a third of seven at Musselburgh nine days ago; 6f will suit but he has over 4l to make up with Furturra on his latest effort and he is drawn out in stall 11.
runners.comment: 6f should suit but comfortably held by Furturra on latest run and poorly drawn

runners.name: Stellar Quest
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros foal; by Space Traveller; third foal; half-brother to winners Solvency (5f 2yo including AW; RPR 83) and Latin Race (Italian 5f 2yo/6f); dam twice-raced (at 5f; 69) half-sister to Qatari 6f-1m winner Mister Staple; represents a top yard and he is one to take seriously starting out in selling company.
runners.comment: 16,000euros half-brother to two winners; powerful 2yo stable; commands serious respect
runners.owner: D J Mackay & West Shaw Farm

runners.name: Claret And Blues
runners.spotlight: He has shown some promise in two 5f novice runs (good ground/Tapeta) and this drop in grade/step up in trip can help; stall 12 no assistance though.
runners.comment: Some promise in two 5f novices but he has the widest stall to contend with this time

runners.name: Stay On Tab
runners.spotlight: Poor form in three 5f runs, the latest behind two reopposing rivals at Musselburgh nine days ago; new trip/tongue-tie not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form in three 5f runs; new trip/tongue-tie not enough to tempt

runners.name: Trust In Far
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and tailed off in a 6f fillies' maiden at Haydock eight days ago (80-1); no appeal.
runners.comment: Offered little at Haydock eight days ago; no appeal

runners.name: Fortunate
runners.spotlight: Poor form in her three starts, looking a difficult ride at Kempton ten days ago; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Poor form in her three starts and she looked a hard ride latest; easy enough to swerve

runners.name: Happy Humpo
runners.spotlight: 12-1 and always in rear when last of 11 at Beverley last month (5f, good to soft); should do better in time and interesting to see how she fares in the market now dropped into a seller; well drawn.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut (5f) but bred to do better and well drawn; check betting

runners.name: Believitanducan
runners.spotlight: Three months off before returning to Flat with a win at Ascot (2m, good; 12 ran) three weeks ago, always prominent as 11-8 favourite; up 4lb and lacks any experience of this track but a huge player if he takes to it.
runners.comment: Back to Flat with a win at Ascot (2m, good) three weeks ago as 11-8 favourite; up 4lb

runners.name: Solar Pass
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been on AW (latest in March) and her only run at this track did not go well, when favourite over 1m6f on heavy on final start last term; close second with first-time cheekpieces at Goodwood (2m, good) on latest outing, however, is a notable positive.
runners.comment: Close second with first-time cheekpieces at Goodwood (2m, good) on latest outing

runners.name: Imperial Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Sole win (19 races) was a novice in 2023; running respectably this term, drawn wide when never able to challenge in the Chester Plate last time; competitively handicapped but probably not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Sole win (19 races) was a novice in 2023; running respectably this term

runners.name: Dance Time
runners.spotlight: 6-19 on AW but 0-10 on turf; none of her turf form matches this mark and her second here last September was on heavy going; blinkered latest; while moving back up from 1m4f should help, others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: None of her turf form matches this mark and her 2nd here last September was on heavy going

runners.name: Savrola
runners.spotlight: Progressive front-runner last year (over 1m6f, 1m5f and 2m) who finished either first or second in all five of those races, but he wasn't seen after July; 30l defeat on recent reappearance (12-1) suggests he's best watched but his trainer had two jumps winners this week.
runners.comment: Progressive front-runner last year but beaten 30l on recent reappearance (12-1)

runners.name: Venezuelan
runners.spotlight: Small-field Lingfield maiden last August was his only win but he was on the premises in nearly all his handicaps; tried various headgear on last five starts and switches it again on this first run since leaving Ralph Beckett for 90,000gns in October; unraced beyond 1m4f but staying trips are worth exploring and he has a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Left R Beckett for 90,000gns October; staying trips are worth exploring; competitive mark
runners.owner: P and Mrs G A Clarke

runners.name: Percy Jones
runners.spotlight: All four wins were on Wolverhampton AW, the latest off a 2lb higher mark last August (1m4f); 0-22 record on turf includes six seconds and two of those (one last year) were at this track; this is his first 2m race for a while but he used to look fully effective over it and, with a run under his belt now this term, he's considered each-way in the first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-22 turf record includes six 2nds (two here); considered each-way in first cheekpieces

runners.name: Heathen
runners.spotlight: 5-19 on AW and 0-9 on turf; twice close up over 1m4f on turf last year, including off this mark at Chester, but is also a dual 1m6f winner on AW and has at least one piece of 2m form in that sphere; a case can be made if he's tuned up sufficiently after 259 days off; the market may guide.
runners.comment: A case can be made if he's tuned up sufficiently after 259 days off

runners.name: Ouro Preto
runners.spotlight: 3-15 when trained in France, the latest win in a claimer at Longchamp (1m4f, very soft) last September; well handicapped judged on peak French efforts but he was a well-beaten last of seven on British debut at Hamilton (1m3f, good; 14-1) 15 days ago; unraced much beyond 1m4f.
runners.comment: Ex-French; well-beaten last on British debut at Hamilton (1m3f, good; 14-1) 15 days ago

runners.name: Midnight Rumble
runners.spotlight: Sole win (22 races) was on AW in June 2024; five seconds on turf and bang there on AW (1m3f/1m4f) on last two starts before 30,000gns sale (left Alan King) in April, but this is the first time he's gone beyond that on Flat.
runners.comment: Sole win (22 races) on AW in 2024; left Alan King for 30,000gns in April; new Flat trip

runners.name: Kuredu King
runners.spotlight: 11.6f and 1m6f handicap winner last year for the Gosdens; however, soundly beaten on both attempts over hurdles for new yard this spring, given wind surgery in between; player if he can tap back into those 2025 performances.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both hurdle runs for new yard this spring, given wind surgery in between

runners.name: Brighton Boy
runners.spotlight: Course winner (7.6f, soft) for Andrew Balding last summer; not beaten far in the 7.6f handicap won by Supido 24 days ago (poor draw) and confirmed that promise with a Class 3 win at Thirsk a fortnight ago (7f, good); a 3lb rise shouldn't prove beyond him and Warren Fentiman's claim is a bonus.
runners.comment: Course winner; led late on at Thirsk 2 weeks ago (7f, good); 3lb higher but should go well

runners.name: Lexington Jet
runners.spotlight: Transformed by blinkers this year, making all in three 1m handicaps at Chelmsford; well drawn to attack but he will need a big personal best to score at this level.
runners.comment: Three 1m wins at Chelmsford, making all on each occasion; Class 2 may find him out

runners.name: Supido
runners.spotlight: Finished close up in fifth in the Britannia Stakes at Royal Ascot last year (1m, good to firm); his 3yo campaign rather fizzled out but he dropped in the weights as a result and took full advantage with his 7.6f success here 24 days ago; well drawn again and a 4lb rise isn't beyond him.
runners.comment: Capitalised on good mark here (7.6f) 24 days ago; 4lb rise shouldn't prove beyond him

runners.name: Elements Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; 4-4 on AW but unplaced in both turf runs; he will need a career best to come out on top but he is well drawn to attack and still has low mileage.
runners.comment: 4-4 on AW but unplaced both turf starts; good draw; not fully exposed

runners.name: Bobby Bennu
runners.spotlight: Course winner; only eighth in the corresponding race 12 months ago; 4lb lower today but he is drawn wide and this may not be his day.
runners.comment: Only 8th in this race last year; 4lb lower today but stall 12 not helpful

runners.name: Midnight Strike
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo for Joseph O'Brien; poorly drawn and finished well beaten in a C&D handicap on stable debut this month (100-1); he has fared better with the draw today but others still look more appealing.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo; well-beaten 100-1 shot in C&D handicap on stable debut; better draw today
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Miami Matrix
runners.spotlight: Two course wins (7.6f) for Hugo Palmer last summer; respectable fourth on this month's stable debut; should give his running but perhaps vulnerable for win purposes.
runners.comment: Two course wins (7.6f) for Hugo Palmer last summer; encouraging stable debut this month

runners.name: Nizam
runners.spotlight: Three wins and boasted some strong form when trained in France; 80-1 and never threatened on his stable debut six weeks ago; Ian Williams looks to have stronger claims elsewhere.
runners.comment: Three-time winner in France; low-key stable debut last month; likely best watched

runners.name: Angel Hunter
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a competitive 1m handicap at Lingfield on Good Friday; two midfield finishes this month and he will need a career best to come out on top in this field.
runners.comment: 1m AW win on Good Friday was a solid effort; 2 midfield finishes since; career best needed

runners.name: The Angel King
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; low-key stable debut at Haydock in April but he fared much better when sixth, beaten under 3l, behind stablemate Supido here (7.6f, good) 24 days ago; he hasn't fared so kindly with the draw this time.
runners.comment: Good run behind stablemate Supido here this month; the draw has not been so kind today

runners.name: Roman Dragon
runners.spotlight: Enhanced his excellent course record when narrowly landing a 5f handicap at the May meeting; stays 7f but rarely seen at the trip now; may prove vulnerable to stronger stayers.
runners.comment: Eighth course win when scoring over 5f three weeks ago; having a rare 7f run; vulnerable

runners.name: Korker
runners.spotlight: Slow-starter; on a dangerous mark but he is without a win since October 2023 and one 7f run didn't see him improve for the trip.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but on a long losing run and stamina for 7f not yet assured
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Palmar Bay
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of a C&D handicap last summer off a 12lb lower mark; better than ever when a close fourth in a Wolverhampton Listed race in March and he faced a stiff task at Haydock three weeks ago; should give his running but could find one or two stronger back in handicap company.
runners.comment: C&D winner; drops back from Listed company but could find one or two stronger

runners.name: Fizzy Cristal
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a 6f win at Newcastle and she was back on the scoresheet over that C&D 11 days ago; up from Class 5 today and her turf efforts leave her with something to find.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins at Newcastle, including Class 5 handicap 11 days ago; turf form lags behind

runners.name: Trust Amy
runners.spotlight: C&D win last September before a good fifth in Listed company at Doncaster (6f, heavy); didn't run badly in another Listed event on last week's reappearance but her handicap runs don't suggest she is the answer, even from the inside stall.
runners.comment: C&D winner but her handicap efforts don't suggest she is the answer today despite stall 1

runners.name: Ruby's Angel
runners.spotlight: Won a big-field nursery (6f, good to firm) at last season's Ebor meeting; good second to Cherry Baker over C&D on her seasonal reappearance and 4lb better off with that rival today; drawn in stall 1 on that occasion but she's widest today which makes it easy enough to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Good 2nd to Cherry Baker here on her reappearance; drawn 1 then but has stall 12 this time

runners.name: Zighy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt as a 2yo, winning a 6f fillies' novice decisively; stronger form when coming from off the pace to win a Class 4 handicap at Southwell last month but she was beaten fair and square at Yarmouth two weeks later (7f, good to firm); return to 6f can help but Champonie may prove the yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Progressive filly who should appreciate the return to 6f; stable also run Champonie
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Saffron Dandy
runners.spotlight: Of interest on this year's AW best; latest Ripon run was underwhelming though and others look open to greater progress.
runners.comment: Chance on this year's AW best but she was disappointing back on turf 13 days ago

runners.name: Slay Queen
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f last summer; low-key return to action at Newmarket four weeks ago (6f, good to firm; handles much slower); still 5lb higher than for her handicap win; vulnerable.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last year but she made a low-key return to action at Newmarket this month

runners.name: Champonie
runners.spotlight: Won 6f AW events on her first two starts; lost her unbeaten record but enhanced her reputation when keeping on for sixth in a 5f handicap here (good ground) three weeks ago; the return to this trip will suit and she remains capable of better.
runners.comment: 2-2 over 6f; found 5f inadequate here three weeks ago; capable of better back at this trip

runners.name: Tarot
runners.spotlight: Placed in her first three 2yo starts; made all to win a 6f fillies' novice at Kempton when last seen in October; bred to carry on progressing and the second and third from that Kempton race won their next start; stall 11 makes life tough though.
runners.comment: Form of 6f AW win last October boosted since; more to come but stall 11 not ideal

runners.name: Hayynah
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when beating eight rivals in a 5f novice at Southwell in February; had earlier chased home Champonie at Newcastle but had an experience advantage that day; needs to bounce back from a low-key Southwell run (5f; handicap debut) seven weeks ago.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Southwell (5f) in February but ran poorly on handicap debut latest

runners.name: Cherry Baker
runners.spotlight: Found the Group 3 Nell Gwyn Stakes all too much for her on her reappearance but she left it behind on her handicap debut, enjoying a charmed run up the inside to score comfortably in a C&D handicap 24 days ago; up 5lb and drawn even wider today; another career best is required.
runners.comment: Cosy C&D win on handicap debut this month; 5lb rise/wider stall demand further progress

runners.name: Evie Ross
runners.spotlight: Easily won a 5f maiden at Chepstow on her second 2yo run; never in the hunt behind Cherry Baker on her seasonal/handicap debut here 24 days ago but the run wasn't without hope, drawn wide in stall 12, racing freely under restraint and not enjoying a clear passage at a crucial time; stall 9 again not ideal but she's one to keep an eye on as the season progresses.
runners.comment: Better than the result here on this month's handicap debut; draw could have been kinder
runners.owner: S R Hope And S W Barrow

runners.name: Folk Pageant
runners.spotlight: Last of nine on handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW; 11-1) in March but won starts either side in a maiden at Lingfield (1m, AW; made all) and eight-runner handicap at Newmarket (1m2f, good; always prominent); the latter was by a short head at 25-1 but she's gone up just 3lb since that rallying display and that does not look harsh.
runners.comment: One poor run from four starts; won two, including Newmarket handicap (1m2f, good); up 3lb
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Crockham Heath
runners.spotlight: Front-running wins in a Southwell novice (1m) and Wolverhampton handicap (9.4f; with authority) in his only two AW races; those were way better than turf performances at Newmarket on debut and in 1m2f handicap (good to firm) on latest outing, which is a concern even though these are early days.
runners.comment: Two front-running wins on AW looked promising but were way better than both turf efforts

runners.name: Gatehouse
runners.spotlight: Bright start to career on AW included a novice win at Chelmsford (1m2f) in December and short-head handicap win at Southwell (1m) in April; however, he was put firmly in his place on turf debut at Newmarket (good to firm) four weeks ago when a return to 1m2f should have suited.
runners.comment: Two AW wins before put firmly in his place on turf debut at Newmarket (good to firm)

runners.name: Mayaada
runners.spotlight: After 12 races, for two wins, she should be far more exposed than today's rivals; however, her best form was on last two outings when second at Epsom (8.5f, good) and winning with a steady late bid at Salisbury (upped to 1m2f, good; 4lb higher today) and that earns respect.
runners.comment: 12 races but best form on last two; won when upped to 1m2f at Salisbury on latest

runners.name: Timber Twelve
runners.spotlight: Made most to win 1m novices on AW at Kempton in September and Southwell in March; that perfect start was followed by a lesser front-running effort at Epsom (8.5f, good) on turf/handicap debut but he looks bred for 1m2f.
runners.comment: 2-2 on AW but a lesser front-running effort at Epsom (8.5f, good) on turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Blue Noon
runners.spotlight: Minor honours in maiden/novice events at Leicester (7f), Newmarket (1m2f) and Salisbury (1m3f); led over 2f out last time but looked green in front and carried her head a bit high; early days, going handicapping first time.
runners.comment: Minor honours each time; green in front latest and carried her head a bit high; early days

runners.name: Advance Twentyfive
runners.spotlight: Second in a maiden here last September; best form in valuable sales races (also 7f) on next two outings but nowhere near repeating it in his two starts this term; hampered early in a York handicap last time (7f; 22-1) and this is a major rethink on his trip, but none too persuasive.
runners.comment: Best form in valuable 7f sales races as 2yo but he's been nowhere near repeating it

runners.name: Bemersyde
runners.spotlight: Minor honours over 1m at Newmarket (good) and Windsor (good to firm) on first two starts, before a lesser show under 7lb claimer on latest outing; one to note stepped up to 1m2f for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Two minor honours before a lesser show latest; interesting upped to 1m2f on handicap debut

runners.name: Home Hero
runners.spotlight: Finished closer each time in his three-race 2yo career and met trouble in running when fourth of nine at Haydock (1m, good) on final outing, looking on an even keel only very late in the day when running on well; gelded the following day; highly likely to stay and he's a fascinating handicap newcomer who needs a close look for any strength behind him in the betting.
runners.comment: A fascinating handicap newcomer who needs a close look for any strength in the betting

runners.name: Knowledge
runners.spotlight: Benefited from the runner-up hanging sharply left when narrow winner of 1m novice at Southwell (AW) in January on debut; outsider of five when he finished last in novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good; bred to stay) seven weeks ago, behind talented rivals but it did not advertise his claims; gelded before this handicap debut.
runners.comment: 1m debut winner on Southwell AW but last of five in Yarmouth novice (1m2f, good); gelded

runners.name: Carwyn
runners.spotlight: Twice showed fair form at 1m in his four Irish races last term for Ger Lyons; well beaten on handicap debut there, however, and it was the same story this month on both British starts.
runners.comment: Well beaten on Irish handicap debut and the same story this month on both British starts

runners.name: Urban Glimpse
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who showed promise in handicaps at around 1m2f last year, finishing runner-up twice on good; shaped as if needing his Goodwood reappearance after a gelding operation and remains one who should be up to winning handicaps this season; non-runner due to unsuitable ground on good to firm at Windsor earlier in the week but should have a big say if allowed to take his chance.
runners.comment: Low-mileage; strong form in handicaps last year and should come on for reappearance

runners.name: Sir William
runners.spotlight: Won at Windsor (1m2f, good) for William Haggas last August; struggled on first two runs for new yard but returned to form when second of seven back at Windsor (11.5f, good) 12 days ago; first-time cheekpieces go on now.
runners.comment: Back on form for new yard when second at Windsor off this mark 12 days ago; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Moonjid
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a breakthrough win at Nottingham (8.3f, good); ran his best race since when fourth back at Nottingham six weeks ago (cheekpieces first time) but failed to build on it when fifth at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) last Friday; well beaten on previous attempt at 1m2f (for another yard).
runners.comment: Came good at Nottingham last autumn but not obviously shaping as if next win is near

runners.name: Mafting
runners.spotlight: Failed to crack it in 2025 handicaps but reappeared to win off a reduced mark in nine-runner race at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) this April; backed that up when second at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) and this is easier than the York Dante meeting handicap he contested last time.
runners.comment: Win and second on fast ground this spring; well held at York latest but this is easier

runners.name: Impartiality
runners.spotlight: Winner of his first three handicaps in 2025, including here (7.5f, good), and also went close at Glorious Goodwood (1m, heavy) off 2lb higher last July; unable to land a serious blow in handicaps at Musselburgh (1m1f) and Thirsk (1m) this spring but this is less competitive and he's too well treated to ignore; first attempt at 1m2f.
runners.comment: Hasn't threatened in two starts this year but well treated if recapturing his 3yo form

runners.name: Sportingsilvermine
runners.spotlight: Did well last spring and was placed five consecutive times last summer/autumn; now well handicapped on those exploits but beaten upwards of 8l on all four outings this year; can't rule out a revival but hard to be confident.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on last year's exploits but well beaten on all four runs this year

races.race_name: Hilary Needler Trophy EBF Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kodi Bear Light
runners.spotlight: Built on her Musselburgh debut second to Lily Agnes runner-up Hickory Lad when making all in 5f Thirsk maiden (good) a fortnight ago; the outside draw isn't ideal but there was plenty to like about the way she got things done at Thirsk and she could have plenty more to come for her leading yard.
runners.comment: Confirmed debut promise when making all at Thirsk (5f) a fortnight ago; should go well

runners.name: Moonlight Tango
runners.spotlight: All the sharper with a run behind her when making all in a C&D maiden (good to soft) 26 days ago; that form isn't anything special but she has the potential for better again.
runners.comment: Left debut behind when making all over C&D but the form isn't anything special

runners.name: Silesia
runners.spotlight: Placed on both starts, showing fair form when runner-up over C&D (good to soft) 26 days ago; may do better again.
runners.comment: Stepped up on debut third when second over C&D 26 days ago; may do better again

runners.name: Rollthedicebaby
runners.spotlight: Soldier's Call filly who made an encouraging start to her career when chasing home subsequent Marygate runner-up Armor Supreme at Goodwood (5f, good) four weeks ago; open to progress.
runners.comment: Chased home subsequent Listed second on 5f Goodwood debut; open to progress

runners.name: Cornish Pol
runners.spotlight: £6,000 daughter of New Bay and the first foal of a twice-raced sister to 7f Group 3 winner/1,000 Guineas runner-up Lady Kaya; bred for further and would be a surprise winner on debut.
runners.comment: Bred for further and would be a surprise winner on debut

runners.name: Cosmic Mystery
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns breeze-up buy; sister to 5f winner Lochaber (RPR 70) and half-sister to Enchanting Empress who was a sharp early 2yo in 2024 with a win in the Listed National Stakes at Sandown; the yard's 2yos have been doing well, including an impressive first-time-out winner in the Wathnan silks at Bath last week; market confidence behind this one could prove significant.
runners.comment: 220,000 gns breeze-up buy; yard had impressive first-time 2yo winner for Wathnan last week

runners.name: The Can Can Queen
runners.spotlight: Left her C&D debut fifth behind when winning at Ripon (6f, good) 13 days ago and the fifth gave the form a boost when going close at Redcar earlier in the week; likely to improve again.
runners.comment: Stepped up a good deal on C&D debut fifth when winning at Ripon; likely to improve again

runners.name: Roots In Touche
runners.spotlight: Bought for £165,000 at the breeze-ups and bred to be sharp, being a sister to 5f winner Ancient State (RPR 75) and half-sister to last year's dual 5f 2yo Listed winner Ipanema Queen; in good hands and needs checking out in the betting.
runners.comment: Half-sister won two 5f Listed races last year; cost £165,000 last month; check betting

runners.name: Crystal Queen
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard but showed promise when chasing home a Royal Ascot-bound colt from the Karl Burke yard in a three-runner race at Ayr (5f, good) ten days ago; likely to improve for stable which has won this twice since 2018.
runners.comment: Might have bumped into a smart one in a small-field Ayr race on debut; likely improver

runners.name: The Resdev Roman
runners.spotlight: 80-1, prominent for long way when midfield on 6f York Dante meeting debut (good) 16 days ago; the drop to 5f could suit on that evidence and she's open to progress.
runners.comment: Prominent long way on 6f York debut and should progress now down at 5f

races.race_name: Beverley Two Year Old Trophy EBF Conditions Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Matteo
runners.spotlight: Coulsty colt who was way too good for his eight rivals (including Ronson) on his C&D debut (good) 37 days ago; presumably saved for this since and a bold show looks assured from the inside stall.
runners.comment: Easy winner on C&D debut last month and bold show looks assured from inside stall

runners.name: Clash Of Hearts
runners.spotlight: Fared best of the newcomers when third past the post (subsequently promoted to second) in 15-runner novice at York (6f, good) on debut 16 days ago; likely to build on that promising start.
runners.comment: Promise when third past post (promoted to second) on 6f York debut; likely to progress

runners.name: Arapaho Gold
runners.spotlight: Pulled 2.5l clear of a next-time-out winner when overcoming inexperience to make a very promising winning debut at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) four weeks ago; looks a surefire improver.
runners.comment: Overcame inexperience on Thirsk debut and the second has won since; surefire improver

runners.name: Wait Geordie
runners.spotlight: On the back of a Bath debut success (5f, good; made all) he was sent off favourite for the Lily Agnes at Chester (5f, good again) but could only manage third after showing speed again; has a visor added now; his outside draw could make life awkward.
runners.comment: Made all on debut before third in Lily Agnes; visor reached for now; drawn widest

runners.name: Big Cigar
runners.spotlight: £140,000 breeze-up purchase who was sent off favourite and made no mistake on 6f Newcastle debut 11 days ago; open to progress for a yard no stranger to success in this.
runners.comment: Debut winner over 6f at Newcastle recently; yard knows what it takes to win this

runners.name: The Dancing Pirate
runners.spotlight: Could manage only fourth of seven when sent off favourite on 5f Newmarket Guineas meeting debut (good to firm) but three winners have come out of that race and he's evidently well regarded by a leading stable which has won two of the last four runnings of this.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on Newmarket debut but the form has worked out and yard does well in this

runners.name: Blue Pete
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns half-brother to some quite useful winners, including as 2yos; will need to be good to make a winning debut at this level but the stable has had some very smart 2yos for these owners in recent years.
runners.comment: Big ask on debut but yard has had some smart 2yos in these silks in recent years

runners.name: Concert Pitch
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D on his first two starts but no match for Matteo on debut and could only manage sixth at Windsor (6f) last week; outsider.
runners.comment: Placed twice over C&D but his form is well short of required standard here

runners.name: Ronson
runners.spotlight: 12l behind Matteo on his C&D debut but was a totally different proposition when 5l winner back here (good to firm) 18 days ago; may not have beaten much but open to further progress for a stable which has really found form.
runners.comment: Well behind Matteo on C&D debut but won by 5l back here since; should be  more to come

runners.name: Martial Order
runners.spotlight: 11-2 when fading into sixth behind Big Cigar on his 6f Newcastle debut 11 days ago; it's of some interest that his very powerful yard is prepared to pitch him at this level but he'll require significant improvement to get heavily involved.
runners.comment: In top hands but only sixth to Big Cigar on C&D debut and lots of improvement needed

runners.name: Urchin
runners.spotlight: Promising third on 1m Ripon debut (good to firm) last summer and returned to make all with ease in a 1m3f novice there (good to soft) six weeks ago; that was a weak race but he could do no more than hack up and there could be plenty more to come.
runners.comment: Made all with ease in 1m3f Ripon novice on return and could be plenty more to come

runners.name: Who's Lope
runners.spotlight: Green on his debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in December; still didn't look the finished article on his recent Salisbury return (1m2f, good) but showed plenty of promise in staying on for 6l second behind a very useful type; the type to keep on improving as his stamina is drawn out (half-brother to useful 1m6f winner).
runners.comment: Left AW debut behind when staying-on second at Salisbury (1m2f); likely to progress again

runners.name: Hamadhan
runners.spotlight: Improved form on final Irish start (about 1m2f; AW) last September; a little below that level in two 1m4f handicaps for new yard and even a return to his best may leave him vulnerable against a couple of progressive 3yos from top stables.
runners.comment: Fairly useful form but might be vulnerable up against two progressive sorts
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Frankies Dream
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a fine 2025 when a six-time winner (from 5f to 7f); ended up with a lot to do when good third of 13 in 1m Thirsk handicap two weeks ago so he's a player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Ended up with lot to do when good third at Thirsk two weeks ago; player off unchanged mark

runners.name: Capital Guarantee
runners.spotlight: Three-time 7f/1m winner in 2025; good C&D runner-up in April but beat only one in 1m Thirsk handicap two weeks ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Good C&D runner-up in April but beat one in Thirsk h'cap since; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Goldmoyne
runners.spotlight: Completed a four-timer on the AW at Lingfield in March; posted another solid effort when fifth of 11 in 7f Newmarket handicap two weeks ago so he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Good fifth of 11 in 7f Newmarket handicap two weeks ago so he can't be ruled out

runners.name: Physique
runners.spotlight: Has rejoined Micky Fenton and not seen to ideal effect under an aggressive ride when third of seven in 7f Yarmouth handicap 17 days ago; can make his presence felt off a 2lb lower mark here.
runners.comment: Not seen to ideal effect when third at Yarmouth 17 days ago; can make his presence felt

runners.name: Diligent Resdev
runners.spotlight: Ayr Bronze Cup winner in 2024 who also posted some good efforts without winning in 2025; beat one on his return in 7f Haydock handicap after nine months off but he should strip sharper now.
runners.comment: Beat one on return in Haydock h'cap after nine months off but he should strip sharper now

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Scored at Newcastle (7f) in February and in good form since until a below-par fifth in 7f Musselburgh handicap nine days ago; he's the sort to bounce back but drawn wide here.
runners.comment: In good form until just fifth at Musselburgh latest; sort to bounce back but drawn wide

runners.name: Roberto Caro
runners.spotlight: Has started 2026 in very good nick, third of six in 6f Southwell handicap last month; not out of things eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Has started 2026 in very good nick, third in Southwell h'cap latest; not out of things

runners.name: Glenfinnan
runners.spotlight: Winless since August 2024 but he's hinted at promise of late, set plenty to do (also slipped on bend) when seventh of 12 in 7f Thirsk handicap two weeks ago; needs considering off a falling mark.
