runners.comment: Hinted at promise of late, set plenty to do when seventh at Thirsk latest; considered

runners.name: Havana Blue
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; back from five months off with respectable sixth of ten in 7f Haydock handicap three weeks ago; can build on it from an easing mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; off five months before a respectable Haydock sixth; can build on it

runners.name: Billyb
runners.spotlight: Signed off for 2025 with 1m win at Southwell; looked rusty after five months off when eighth of nine in 1m Pontefract handicap last month; took this prize in 2024 so not dismissed with usual cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Rusty on return when eighth at Pontefract last month; took this in 2024 so not dismissed

runners.name: Bell Shot
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with 6f success at Southwell and comes here on the back of a good third of 11 in 7f Kempton handicap; this three-time C&D scorer must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Good third in 7f Kempton handicap; this three-time C&D scorer must enter calculations
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Vietnorm
runners.spotlight: Gelded and much improved when gaining a first win in 1m2f handicap at Ripon 22 days ago, collaring Bradbury on post; merits serious consideration despite 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Gained first win at Ripon latest, collaring Bradbury on post; merits serious consideration

runners.name: Bradbury
runners.spotlight: Progressive form since switched to handicaps this season, though he hung and idled in front before being caught post when a short-head second of eight to Vietnorm at Ripon (1m2f) 22 days ago; 1lb better off here so big shout.
runners.comment: Hung in front when second to Vietnorm at Ripon latest; 1lb better off here so big shout

runners.name: Clear Above
runners.spotlight: Proving a consistent sort and comes here on the back of a solid third of seven in 1m Ripon handicap 22 days ago; not out of things on his first go over this trip.
runners.comment: Solid third at Ripon 22 days ago; not out of things on his first go over this trip

runners.name: Wadacre Geisha
runners.spotlight: Study Of Man filly who took a step forward on her handicap debut when second of nine over C&D 18 days ago, collared only late on; well in the mix nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Improved with C&D second 18 days ago, collared late on; well in the mix nudged up 1lb

races.race_name: Platinum Queen Play Live After Racing Handicap

runners.name: Copper And Five
runners.spotlight: Veteran C&D scorer who slipped in the weights in the second half of last year and arrives in fine form; runner-up in 14-runner event here last month; merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Veteran C&D scorer; in fine form, runner-up here last month; merits serious consideration
runners.owner: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory but he comes here on the back of a good second of eight in 1m handicap at Hamilton 15 days ago; not out of things.
runners.comment: Yet to win but good second at Hamilton 15 days ago; not out of things
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Oilisa
runners.spotlight: Scored twice at 1m last autumn but she's yet to fire in 1m handicaps at Doncaster and Ripon this season; needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Scored twice at 1m last autumn; yet to fire in 1m h'caps at Doncaster and Ripon this term

runners.name: Maple
runners.spotlight: In winning form over hurdles this spring and he posted a good third of nine in C&D handicap last month; enters calculations.
runners.comment: In winning form over hurdles and good third over C&D last month; enters calculations
runners.owner: R Whitelock, L Clemitson & R Jefferson

runners.name: Far Ahead
runners.spotlight: Largely out of sorts this season, beating just two in 1m Ripon handicap last time; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Largely out of sorts this season, beating just two in 1m Ripon handicap last time
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Bullington Bry
runners.spotlight: 0-20; tried in different headgear when a below-par seventh in 7f Doncaster handicap last time out; usual cheekpieces are refitted.
runners.comment: 0-20; tried in different headgear when seventh in 7f Doncaster handicap last time

runners.name: Evelyn's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Nottingham (1m) in April but his form has dipped of late, just sixth in 1m Ayr handicap last time; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways at Nottingham (1m) in April but his form has dipped of late

runners.name: Langholm
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 below par and yet to hit top form this season, well held in 7f Catterick handicap last time; this course winner needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 below par; yet to hit top form this term; more needed from this course winner

runners.name: Elettaria
runners.spotlight: Scored twice over 7f in 2025 but she's failed to make the frame this season, eighth in 7f Redcar handicap five days ago.
runners.comment: Scored twice over 7f in 2025 but she's failed to make the frame this season

runners.name: Caledonian Dream
runners.spotlight: Off for 11 months since a good second in 1m2f handicap here on his final run for Alan Brown; has fitness to prove for his new handler.
runners.comment: Off for 11 months since good second in 1m2f handicap here on his final run for Alan Brown

runners.name: Penny Ghent
runners.spotlight: On the up since front-running tactics were adopted and comfortably landed handicaps at Ripon and over C&D this month; not dismissed lightly in his hat-trick bid saddling a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Comfortably landed handicaps at Ripon and over C&D; not dismissed lightly in hat-trick bid

runners.name: Anificas Beauty
runners.spotlight: Comes here under a cloud, last of seven in 1m4f handicap at Pontefract eight days ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Comes here under a cloud, last of seven in 1m4f handicap at Pontefract eight days ago

runners.name: Langton Wold
runners.spotlight: Capable of fair form but he hasn't been seen out since runner-up over fences at Catterick 17 months ago; others are preferred on this occasion.
runners.comment: Capable of fair form but not seen out since second over fences at Catterick 17 months ago
runners.owner: Brian Ellison Racing Club & M Dale

races.race_name: Anglo Printers Remembering John H. Kierans Mares Hurdle

runners.name: Royal Hollow
runners.spotlight: 3-14 over hurdles and rated 128; won a Clonmel beginners' chase on her penultimate start (15.5f, yld-sft); modest third in mares' listed hurdle at Killarney latest (2m1f, gd-yld); solid place chance on the figures but more needed to win.
runners.comment: 3-14 over hurdles, rated 128, modest Listed third latest, solid place chance

runners.name: Murcia
runners.spotlight: Top-rated here at 138; won Grade 1 at Aintree in April 2025, her last start on ground with good in the description; below best in hot handicaps this year but better expected now back on a sounder surface; giving weight away to most of her rivals but the one to beat.
runners.comment: Aintree Gr'1 winner, below best on soft-heavy this year, return to sound surface huge plus

runners.name: Grainne A Chroi
runners.spotlight: Talented mare has finished out of the first three just twice in six starts over hurdles; won a Grade 3 at Punchestown in February (2m4f, soft); pulled up in Fairyhouse Grade 2 latest; best form on soft ground so rain would boost claims.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner, pulled up in Grade 2 latest, best form on soft ground, needs rain
runners.owner: Ms Eileen O'Brien & John Anthony Harrington

runners.name: Swing Davis
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; landed Navan maiden hurdle by 8.5l in March 2025; failed to complete three starts since, in a Grade 1 mares' novice at Fairyhouse (pulled up) and in beginners' chases at Down Royal (pulled up) and back at Fairyhouse (unseated); questions to answer on return to hurdling.
runners.comment: Failed to complete thrice since maiden hurdle win, questions to answer now

runners.name: Run For Mahler
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; soundly beaten in Grade 2 novice at Cheltenham when last sighted in October; ran well in a maiden hurdle over C&D last September; lacks a recent run and probably best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner, off since modest Gr'2 run in Oct', likely best watched

runners.name: Chosen Comrade
runners.spotlight: Bumper, point-to-point and maiden hurdle winner; limitations seemingly exposed in Listed mares' novice at Punchestown and in the Mares' Novice at Cheltenham; rated 120 and improvement required.
runners.comment: Limitations seemingly exposed the last twice, rated 120, improvement required

runners.name: On A Diamond
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in Clonmel bumper on debut and in a 4yo Cork maiden hurdle on latest; impossible to make a case for.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a bumper and Cork maiden hurdle, impossible to make a case for

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: 3-28 over hurdles but rated just 98 and has been out of form for some time; would be a surprise winner in this company.
runners.comment: 3-28 over hurdles and rated 98, out of form, would be a surprise winner

races.race_name: Join The Listowel Races Supporters Club Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Small Fry
runners.spotlight: Five Flat wins in UK and ran respectably in two autumn Flat starts for this yard after 100,000gns purchase; previous efforts over hurdles were modest but there was promise in his run at Down Royal (2m2f, good) earlier this month when staying on for fourth; entitled to improve over that return; contender.
runners.comment: Five-time Flat winner was a promising fourth over hurdles on seasonal return, contender

runners.name: Penny Express
runners.spotlight: Placed three times in maiden hurdles, including over C&D at this meeting last season; jumped left throughout when pulled up at Clonmel on seasonal return; place chance if his consistency returns.
runners.comment: Placed thrice in m'dens inc' over C&D; PU on return; place chance if consistency returns

runners.name: Bells Grove
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three 2025 bumper runs for John McConnell; could only manage eighth at Kilbeggan on stable debut; needs to return to his best now, making his hurdles bow.
runners.comment: Placed in all three 2025 bumpers but could only manage eighth on stable debut

runners.name: Touch The Moon
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on the Flat on good/good to firm ground, rated 90; some top runs in defeat over hurdles; beaten a short head at Navan in September and third behind the classy Talk The Talk at Limerick next time; soft ground likely against him at Cheltenham in November; positive return to action on the level at Leopardstown last time when second; big player.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner has posted some top runs in defeat over hurdles; big player

runners.name: Gomez Addams
runners.spotlight: Impressed with an easy bumper win for Andy Slattery at the Galway festival last season, with a subsequent Listed winner back in third; ran disappointingly on stable debut at Leopardstown over Christmas (2m, good to yielding); he's better than that; interesting contender making his hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Impressive debut bumper winner disappointed on stable debut; can do better on hurdles bow

runners.name: Mc Swagger
runners.spotlight: Close third in the second of his three bumpers, so has ability; faced a tough task in a Killarney novice contest when unseating at the last (2m4f, good to yielding, 125-1); others preferred.
runners.comment: Showed ability in a bumper when a close third; tough task when UR latest; others preferred

runners.name: Phoenix Arizona
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in points; placed in two small-field maiden hurdles last year; beaten 23l at Kilbeggan when last seen over hurdles; tailed off over fences back there last time; hood goes on but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-7 in points and hard to fancy on form under Rules in this company

runners.name: Dar Toungi
runners.spotlight: Eight Flat wins when trained in France, including at Listed level; nowhere near that form in three runs on the level for this yard; likely best watched on hurdles debut unless money comes.
runners.comment: Eight Flat wins in France; nothing like that in three Flat runs for new yard; check market

runners.name: Forlio
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner is rated 62 in that sphere; some okay runs on the level recently; much more needed to make immediate impact now over hurdles.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner is rated 62 in that sphere; makes hurdling debut

runners.name: Pinkarizon
runners.spotlight: 17,000euros yearling was purchased for 40,000gns as a 2yo; half-brother to winners Rajinsky (7f-2m2f inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 116), Bakhit (1m2f inc AW; 70) and Karamazov (Slovakian 1m 2yo); dam maiden from 5.7f-1m1f (60), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Boomshackerlacker; hooded and tongue tied for debut.
runners.comment: 40,000gns 2yo; half-brother to three Flat winners inc' Rajinsky; hooded/tongue tied

runners.name: Suresuresure
runners.spotlight: Has shown nothing of note in five bumpers; hard to make a case for on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Has shown nothing of note in five bumpers; hard to make a case for on hurdles debut

runners.name: Calzaghi
runners.spotlight: Won a Flat handicap in 2024; tailed off on stable/hurdles debut at Killarney; likeley to be of more interest in handicaps.
runners.comment: 2024 Flat winner was tailed off on stable/hurdles debut; of more interest in h'caps

runners.name: Ballinvasas Best
runners.spotlight: Some promise in his 2024 maiden runs but can only be watched returning from a 645-day absence.
runners.comment: Some promise in 2024 m'dens but can only be watched returning from long absence

runners.name: Straightenyourhand
runners.spotlight: Sent off 100-1 at Killarney on debut and ran like it.
runners.comment: Sent off 100-1 at Killarney on debut and ran like it

runners.name: Beechingstoke
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and pulled up in a Fairyhouse bumper in December; can only be watched after that.
runners.comment: PU in a Fairyhouse bumper in Dec'; can only be watched

runners.name: Approval
runners.spotlight: Progressive 3yo for William Haggas in 2024, winning twice over 1m2f on good to firm; hasn't reached those heights since and was tailed off at Killarney on hurdles/stable debut; hood worn that day now left off.
runners.comment: Progressive 3yo in 2024 on Flat; not at same level since; tailed off on hurdle/stable bow

runners.name: Desertmore Charm
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in a bumper and two maiden hurdles; unlikely.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in a bumper and two maiden hurdles; unlikely

runners.name: Withabitofluk
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on good to yielding; 0-5 over hurdles and rated 113; leading claims on the form of his maiden hurdle runs at Naas and Navan last year but bitterly disappointing in both starts in handicaps in 2026; huge chance if he can rediscover best but comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, big chance on best form but bitterly disappointing last twice, risky

runners.name: Jack Owen Jack
runners.spotlight: Solid efforts when runner-up in bumper at Cork (2m3f, good) and when close third at Kilbeggan after a break on latest (18.5f, good); trip and ground fine and while his dam is flat-bred, he's by a top-class national hunt sire; interesting on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Promise in both bumpers, by top national hunt sire, interesting hurdling debutant

runners.name: Black Dakota
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; modest effort on Rules' debut in a novice hunter chase at Wexford (RPR 99); probably up against it on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Point winner, modest effort in novice hunter chase on Rules' bow, others appeal more

runners.name: Burning Ability
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of three starts in bumpers; good second in a Limerick maiden hurdle in December (2m3f, sft-hvy); off since tailed off in Limerick bumper in February; a repeat of his last run over hurdles would give him a squeak here.
runners.comment: Good second on last start over hurdles in Limerick maiden, chance with a repeat

runners.name: Speed For Fun
runners.spotlight: 1-9 on the Flat in France, the win coming over 1m2f (soft); not the easiest to assess from a form perspective but leading yard usually does well with French recruits and no surprise to see him make an immediate impact; the market should prove informative.
runners.comment: 1-9 on the Flat in France, yard usually does well with French recruits, market informative

runners.name: Ajp Falco
runners.spotlight: No impact in bumpers at Fairyhouse and Punchestown; beaten when falling on hurdles debut at Clonmel (33-1); others preferred.
runners.comment: 33-1 and beaten when falling on hurdles debut at Clonmel, others preferred

runners.name: Pegasuslane
runners.spotlight: 200-1 and tailed off on debut in a Limerick bumper; hard to fancy on that evidence.
runners.comment: 200-1 and tailed off in Limerick bumper, hard to fancy on that evidence

runners.name: Royal Approval
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner on the Flat; okay efforts in maiden hurdles at Navan and Limerick last autumn without really threatening to win; will qualify for a handicap mark after this.
runners.comment: 3-5 on AW on Flat, okay efforts in first two maiden hurdles, gets h'cap mark after this

runners.name: Carajillo
runners.spotlight: 50-1 but offered something to build on when making modest late gains into midfield at Kilbeggan; this longer trip a plus but was beaten 30l last time so much more needed.
runners.comment: 50-1 but flicker of promise on Kilbeggan debut when beaten 30l, more needed

runners.name: Mollys Dolly
runners.spotlight: Bounced back from two poor runs when an 8.75l fifth in a moderate Wexford maiden hurdle (2m4f, good to yielding) last time; more needed.
runners.comment: Fifth in a moderate m'den hurdle last time was an improvement; more needed

runners.name: Jaadil
runners.spotlight: Progressive in the autumn; runner-up at Navan (2m3f, yielding) before gaining a first win in emphatic fashion at Cork (2m3f, good); good effort at Sligo next time when runner-up; reported to have mucus when pulled up on seasonal return at Cork last time; chance at her best.
runners.comment: Progressive last autumn and excuses when PU on seasonal return; chance at her best

runners.name: Dunany Point
runners.spotlight: Won off 6lb lower at Bellewstown last April (20.5f, good); struggled on softer ground in opening two starts this year and good ground at Sligo brought just modest improvement when sent off 11-2 j-fav'; something to prove now.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb lower last April; struggled in three starts this year; something to prove now

runners.name: Sean Mc
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win came at Wexford in April over this trip (good to yielding) off 6lb lower; never counted over 3m1f at Kilbeggan a fortnight later; drop back to this trip should suit; player.
runners.comment: Breakthrough came at Wexford over this trip; never counted over 3m1f latest; player

runners.name: Poppycon
runners.spotlight: Standout run over hurdles when good second in a novice handicap at Wexford in March (2m, soft) on handicap debut; beaten when faller two out over 2m4f at Tramore last month but not ruled out if handling this better ground; tongue tie goes on.
runners.comment: Good second on h'cap debut but beaten when fell last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Mr Eastwood
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability in maiden hurdles but poor efforts in two chases and on handicap hurdle debut at Limerick (2m5f, heavy); cheekpieces go on but others more likely.
runners.comment: Some ability in m'dens but poor in two chases and h'cap hurdle debut; others more likely

runners.name: Doyen Og
runners.spotlight: Good third at Bellewstown (2m1f, good) in August off 2lb higher but didn't build on that in subsequent runs; makes seasonal return now.
runners.comment: Good third at Bellewstown in Aug' but didn't build on that afterwards

runners.name: Le Fanu
runners.spotlight: 0-17 but has run okay at times; 6l fourth of 14 at Limerick (2m4f, soft to heavy) last time was positive but best form is on softer ground.
runners.comment: 0-17 but has run okay at times; best form appears to be on softer

runners.name: Strand Street Lass
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete either point-to-point and tailed off in completed runs under rules; impossible to fancy; blinkers added to tongue tie now; declared for 7.00 at Tramore on Friday.
runners.comment: Failed to complete either point and tailed off in completed starts under rules

runners.name: Johnny M
runners.spotlight: First reserve; winless from ten starts and returned from a long absence when tailed off at Punchestown on stable debut; new headgear tried now.
runners.comment: Reserve; 0-10 and tailed off back from a long absence on stable debut

runners.name: Jay Pee M
runners.spotlight: Attracted heavy market support at Cork last time (2m3f, good) but could only manage fifth; punters likely attracted by now 19lb lower hurdles mark, but dual chase winner is yet to match that form in this sphere; may want softer ground than this.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner holds 19lb lower hurdles mark but has struggled to take advantage of it

runners.name: Tellmemamema
runners.spotlight: Outran 80-1 odds at Down Royal (2m2f, good) on handicap debut earlier this month when fourth of 20, staying on well; this trip could bring further improvement.
runners.comment: Outran 80-1 odds on h'cap debut when staying on for fourth; this trip could bring more

runners.name: Sonny Fella
runners.spotlight: Yet to trouble the judge in 14 starts; beaten 18l in a maiden hurdle (2m, good to yielding) at Kilbeggan last time and handicap runs are not encouraging either.
runners.comment: Yet to trouble the judge in 14 starts; beaten 18l in a m'den last time

runners.name: Move It On Over
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15l at Ballinrobe last August on handicap debut (2m1f, good, 100-1); well beaten at Down Royal on seasonal return (2m2f, good); entitled to come on from that but others preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Down Royal on seasonal return; others preferred

runners.name: Where Are You Now
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; well-backed for handicap debut at Punchestown (2m, soft to heavy) when a costly mistake at two out saw him beaten by a head after rallying; couldn't justify favouritism upped in trip back at Punchestown (3m, good to yielding) last time.
runners.comment: Reserve; beaten a head on h'cap debut over 2m; well beaten upped in trip last time

runners.name: Loughrask Jet
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on handicap debut at Thurles (2m4f, heavy) and better ground didn't bring much improvement at Kilbeggan last time (2m3f, good to yielding).
runners.comment: Tailed off on h'cap debut and better ground didn't bring improvement last time

runners.name: Sekakarma
runners.spotlight: Showed notable improvement in his second handicap start at Punchestown (2m, good to yielding) when weakening into fifth of 18; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Notable improvement came on second h'cap start when fifth; needs to build on that

runners.name: Bravemans Bounce
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; three maiden runs don't give much encouragement, but could improve now handicapping.
runners.comment: Reserve; Not much enouragement in m'dens but could improve now h'capping

runners.name: Illtakehimmyself
runners.spotlight: Best run came on second handicap start at Punchestown (good to yielding) last time when upped to 3m for the first time and beaten a short head off 3lb lower; the abandonment of the first-time tongue tie worn that day and the drop back in trip are both question marks now but good chance with a repeat of latest run.
runners.comment: Upped to 3m last time when beaten a short head; drops back in trip and tongue tie left off

runners.name: Ask The Bookie
runners.spotlight: 0-19 under rules; fairly modest form last season; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-19 under rules and fairly modest form last season; others preferred.

runners.name: King Ferdinand
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner is best watched on return from such a long absence unless market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Best watched on return from such long absence unless market speaks

runners.name: High Court Cave
runners.spotlight: Point and bumper winner added a hurdles win at Downpatrick in September (2m2f, yielding); creditable return to action at Punchestown earlier this month when seventh of 24 (2m, yielding), shaping as though he would improve; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Creditable return to action at Punchestown; not ruled out with improvement

runners.name: Elusive Ogie
runners.spotlight: Limerick maiden hurdle winner this time last year (2m1f, good); travelling smoothly and looked likely to be involved in the finish when sprawling and unseating at the second last at Limerick last time off this mark (2m4f, good to yielding); lightly raced and dangerous if repeating; good third in the Lartigue (2m, good to yielding) in other run at this track.
runners.comment: Travelled smoothly when UR at the second last at Limerick; dangerous with a repeat

runners.name: Chanceawetmorning
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten from two starts since joining this yard; well-backed to win a Kilbeggan handicap in April (2m1f, good to yielding) and followed up with today's rider aboard at Clonmel next time; made to work harder to win last time and is up another 7lb, but this step up in trip could unlock a bit more.
runners.comment: Unbeaten from two since joining this yard; up 7lb more but maybe this trip can unlock more

runners.name: Big Dispute
runners.spotlight: Struggled after winning a bumper on debut; staged a revival last summer when placed in three maiden hurdles; well backed and ran creditably in a Cork rated novice hurdle last time when third (2m2f, good); getting the hang of things and should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Bumper winner has been getting the hang of things over hurdles recently; thereabouts

runners.name: Rockonliam
runners.spotlight: Killarney maiden hurdle winner (2m4f, yielding) last season, beating Big Dispute by 0.75l; cruised easily to the lead before emptying late on at Leopardstown (2m5f, good to yielding) when last seen over Christmas; that performance suggests a win off this mark is within reach but may improve for this return.
runners.comment: Cruised to the lead before emptying at Leopardstown over Christmas, chance

runners.name: Therellbguddaysyet
runners.spotlight: Good ground Thurles maiden hurdle winner who has been placed off this mark; ran a decent race faced with an unrealistic task at Cork on stable debut and another positive effort at Killarney when fifth back in handicap company (4.25l behind Falco Blitz); blinkers go on and he's lightly-raced for his age.
runners.comment: M'den hurdle winner has run creditably in both starts for this yard; lightly-raced for age

runners.name: Tir Og
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well in the early part of last season, rising 30lb in the ratings, stemming from three wins and two seconds; probably too high in the ratings now; unsuited by race conditions at Killarney when tailed off last time; others more likely.
runners.comment: Progressed really well early last season but probably too highly rated now

runners.name: Earl Neigh
runners.spotlight: Some promise in his maiden over 2m-2m4f on varying ground; always behind when upped to 3m2f for his handicap debut at Sligo; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Some promise in m'dens but PU when upped to 3m2f at Sligo on h'cap bow

runners.name: Hamsiyann
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner's sole success over hurdles came at the Cheltenham November meeting in 2024 when successful off 7lb lower (2m5f, good); beaten 31l in a quality Aintree handicap in April (2m1f, good to soft) when last seen over obstacles; run on the Flat at Chester wasn't too encouraging either.
runners.comment: Flat and hurdles winner hasn't given too much encouragement in two recent outings

runners.name: Bop Wont Stop
runners.spotlight: Creditable return from over a year off at Cork (2m3f, good) when a 9l fourth of 15, hampered and made a mistake late on; in the mix if backing it up.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth on return from over a year off; in the mix if backing it up

runners.name: One And Only
runners.spotlight: Added a second hurdles win at Limerick (2m7f, heavy) in March off 6lb lower;  couldn't back it up at Cork (3m, yielding) last time when a 16.5l eighth; others preferred.
runners.comment: Added second hurdles win at Limerick in March but couldn't back it up last time

runners.name: Falco Blitz
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner (four chases, two hurdles) holds a 14lb lower hurdles mark and ran a very encouraging race returning from an absence at Killarney when third; entitled to improve on that; interesting.
runners.comment: Holds a 16lb lower hurdles mark and was an encouraging third back from long absence latest

runners.name: Garnacho
runners.spotlight: Justified strong market support when scoring by 12l on debut for this yard at Bellewstown (2m5f, good) in July; has struggled off 16lb higher mark in three runs since; may need some help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner on stable debut; struggled off 16lb higher mark in three runs since

races.race_name: In Memory Of Josephine Lyons 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Somersby Roost
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner made the breakthrough under Rules at Cork (2m3f, good) in her second handicap with an easy 7l win; given a sizeable 10lb hit for that but has scope to improve up in trip with the ground to suit.
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough at Cork with an easy win; up 10lb but scope to improve up in trip

runners.name: Rhythmic Tune
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m5f Limerick handicap on yielding ground last October but moderate in three runs since; needs to return to his best; remains 4lb above winning mark.
runners.comment: Limerick 2m5f h'cap winner in Oct' but moderate in three runs since; must return to best

runners.name: Shesakindofmagic
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in 2024 (yielding to soft); beaten 22l in seventh on handicap debut at Cork (2m3f, good) last time, jumping lacking fluency; more needed.
runners.comment: Beaten 22l on h'cap debut last time, jumping lacking fluency; more needed

runners.name: Its Not Over Yet
runners.spotlight: Won two 3m hurdles in 2024; some fine runs in defeat last year, but was poor on chase debut making her return at Limerick; some improvement back over hurdles at Bellewstown last time, but improvement required.
runners.comment: Dual winner in 2024; okay run last time but improvement required

runners.name: Only For Our Man
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner got off the mark in a 2m6f novice handicap (good) at Down Royal last month with an easy 7l victory; good run off 7lb higher under today's rider when beaten a head at Cork (3m1f, good); up another 4lb and enters higher grade, but big player with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Easy winner at Down Royal was beaten a head upped in trip last time; up 4lb but big player

runners.name: Brideswell Lad
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner (two over hurdles) but not since May 2024; missed the entirety of 2025; hasn't convinced in runs this year, both over fences and hurdles; holds 27lb lower rating compared to chasing but others preferred.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner hasn't convinced in runs this year since return from absence

runners.name: Way Down South
runners.spotlight: Point/maiden hurdle/beginners' chase winner; more fluent jumping in the final stages at Limerick (2m6f, soft) would have seen him go closer than the 2.25l losing margin; tapped for toe at Cork (2m3f, good) last time back in trip when third; return to this distance is a positive; in the mix.
runners.comment: Went close at Limerick and tapped for toe back in trip at Cork; in the mix

runners.name: Embrace The Day
runners.spotlight: Promise when winning a point for Jonathan Fogarty; didn't show much in first five hurdle starts but best effort yet at Kilbeggan (19.5f, good to yielding) in first-time tongue-tie (retained) when fourth of 15; got tired late on when upped to 2m6f (soft) at Limerick on Saturday; stamina query now.
runners.comment: Best effort yet at Kilbeggan on penultimate, got tired late upped to 2m6f latest

runners.name: Whatsavailable
runners.spotlight: His three hurdle wins came at Fairyhouse on good ground at 2m4f-3m but nothing since late 2024; some cracking runs in defeat though, including when third off this mark at Cork (3m, yielding); challenging for the lead when unseated at the third last at Wexford last time; down to an attractive mark.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner was a good third at Cork and challenging when UR latest

runners.name: Silent Guardian
runners.spotlight: Scored on second handicap hurdle start over 2m3f at Clonmel in March (soft) off 99 when dropped in grade; moderate on the Flat at Roscommon 12 days ago; this is a tougher task than his win but can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Scored on second h'cap hurdle start in a lower grade at Clonmel; moderate on Flat latest

runners.name: Ceroc
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner was poor at Wexford in April off 4lb higher (2m4f, good to yielding) but rebounded over fences back there last time when a distant second of 11 (3m2f, good to yielding); can't be ruled out but others are more appealing.
runners.comment: Rebounded from poor hurdles run when second over fences latest; others more appealing

races.race_name: Oliver Broderick (Pro/Am) Fillies INH Flat Race

runners.name: Tell The Boys
runners.spotlight: Promise when an 8.5l fourth on debut in Wexford bumper (2m, soft); better again when beaten 2.25l on good ground at Cork (16.5f) in first-time hood (retained) and that form has been well advertised by the third who won next time; leading form claims.
runners.comment: Promise on debut, even better at Cork latest, form has been boosted, leading claims

runners.name: The President
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park filly the first foal out of top class racemare Benie Des Dieux; plenty of appeal on paper and represents a top yard; very interesting debutante; market can guide.
runners.comment: Walk In The Park filly, out of top racemare Benie Des Dieux, very interesting debutante

runners.name: Velvet Ash
runners.spotlight: Sholokhov filly; E8,000 3yo; dam unraced half-sister to winners Fury Road (high-class bumper and 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase) and Monbeg Worldwide (useful bumper/2m4f hurdle); respected source and nicely bred; market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: Nicely bred Sholokhov filly, dam related to bumper winners, market can guide on debut

runners.name: Don'trushraven
runners.spotlight: Workforce filly; third foal; dam point and 2m-2m6f hurdle winner, out of point winning half-sister to bumper winner Chunky and 1m2f Flat/2m hurdle winner Quill Project; yard had a recent bumper winner at Kilbeggan; check the market.
runners.comment: Workforce filly, dam point and hurdles winner, yard had recent bumper win at Kilbeggan
runners.owner: Kenneth Oliver Rushe & Mrs Anne Gleeson

runners.name: Lake Pippaway
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete both starts in points on easy ground; Getaway filly's dam was a useful bumper winner and liked good ground so could improve; worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in two points, dam a useful bumper winner, could improve, check market

runners.name: Lavender Berry
runners.spotlight: Maxios filly; half-sister to winners Pilot Show (3m/3m1f chase) and Cuchcullan's Son (point); dam unraced half-sister to winners Sa Fureur (smart 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and King's Odyssey (useful 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase); may need further than this in time; watch.
runners.comment: Maxios filly, half-sister to 3m-3m1f winner, may need further in time, watch

runners.name: Lily Lovelace
runners.spotlight: Ulysses filly; 5,000gns foal; half-sister to winners Frivola (Italian Listed-placed 7.5f 2yo) and Charming Look (Spanish 7.5f-10.5f); dam Italian 1m-1m3f winner (inc 2yo/Listed; RPR 103); likely best watched on debut (yard 0-8 in bumpers last 5 seasons).
runners.comment: Ulysses filly, dam Italian 1m-1m3f winner, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Vadique
runners.spotlight: Vadamos filly; half-sister to 2m3f hurdle winner Mr Finch; dam unraced sister to 2m hurdle/chase winner Stromstad and French 1m4f winner Maelstrom; worth a market check on debut for shrewd yard.
runners.comment: Vadamos filly, half-sister to 2m3f hurdles winner, shrewd yard, check the market

runners.name: Born To Caledonie
runners.spotlight: Beaten 17l on debut on testing ground at Leopardstown; no improvement for better ground at Down Royal on latest; may have needed that but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Modest efforts in bumpers at Leopardstown and Down Royal, others appeal more

races.race_name: Pennine Brewing Company Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Skip To Victory
runners.spotlight: All three runs have been over 5f at Southwell, best effort when beaten a head in February; turf debut (half-sister to a Listed turf winner); chance.
runners.comment: Placed in two of three runs over 5f at Southwell; makes turf debut; one with a chance
runners.owner: On Top Racing, J T Elliott, S Thomas

runners.name: Vinciamo
runners.spotlight: Third in a Newbury maiden (5f, good) in April last year on only start for Marcus Tregoning; subsequently gelded and absent for 11 months; third at Newcastle on stable debut in March (5f) but well beaten on Tapeta last time (5f); back after a wind op with tongue-tie on; a possible.
runners.comment: Third in a Newbury maiden in April 2025; not done quite as well since; back after wind op

runners.name: Stoneacre Joe
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on soft ground at Haydock last week, but comes into the reckoning on his two previous runs when second in a Doncaster novice (6f, good to firm) and in an Ayr maiden (6f, good to firm); speedy and should be as effective at this trip; worth considering.
runners.comment: Disappointing on soft ground last week but chance on his two previous runs on good to firm

runners.name: Forest Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Has shown modest form over 6f/7f and likely to be one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Has shown modest form over 6f/7f and is likely to be one for handicaps

runners.name: Gwen Tennyson
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maiden/novice on the AW over 5f/6f and best watched for now.
runners.comment: Ordinary form in maiden/novice on the AW over 5f/6f and best watched for now

runners.name: Aurora's Doublesix
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden who has a very stiff task in this novice event.
runners.comment: 14-race maiden who has a very stiff task in this novice event

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: You Mystify Me
runners.spotlight: Placed behind a couple of smart sorts on first two runs (5f, good to firm/good), although the overall form didn't amount to a great deal; makes handicap debut after poor run last time (6f, good); worth a market check.
runners.comment: Poor run latest, but placed on two previous starts; worth a market check on handicap debut
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing

runners.name: O Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Best of his seven runs was on second handicap start when 3.25l fifth of nine at Nottingham last time (5f, good; cheekpieces on for first time and retained); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Best of seven runs was last time when tried in cheekpieces (retained); each-way chance

runners.name: Powernap
runners.spotlight: Hasn't run up to her best on her two runs this year, latest on the AW, having shaped with a degree of promise in a couple of nurseries last October; tongue-tie now tried; well drawn.
runners.comment: Not run up to her best in 2026 having shaped quite well in nurseries last term; good draw

runners.name: Havin A Flyer
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; would have a sound chance on her two seconds at Musselburgh off much higher marks last August/September but didn't progress on the AW in the winter; could go well back on turf after a 130-day break.
runners.comment: Two sound runs in turf h'caps last year; on a good mark now; chance after 130-day break

runners.name: Flybar
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts over 6f/7f last term, including in two nurseries, with a visor tried on final start (7f); on a lowly mark, and with her stable gong well, is one to consider on first try at 5f.
runners.comment: Modest efforts over 6f/7f last term; worth considering after a break on first try at 5f

runners.name: I'm Dan Dare
runners.spotlight: Ex-Adrian Nicholls; best run when beaten a nose at Redcar earlier this month (6f, good; cheekpieces on for first time); has changed yards after a disappointing run last time; one to consider despite the drop in trip.
runners.comment: Ex-Adrian Nicholls; nose second two runs back (headgear tried); chance on stable debut

runners.name: Sea Her Excel
runners.spotlight: Strong-puller; best form when 3.75l third of three in a Newcastle maiden last September; subsequently given wind surgery, but well beaten at Nottingham on handicap debut last month (6f, good; hood tried but now discarded); drop to 5f could suit.
runners.comment: Strong puller; some promise but well beaten on h'cap debut after wind op; 5f could suit

runners.name: Moretons
runners.spotlight: Ex-George Scott; has run a couple of fair races for new yard, notably when 2l fifth of seven at Musselburgh in April (5f, good); overall has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Ex-George Scott; has run a couple of good races for new yard; overall has a bit to prove

runners.name: With Glory
runners.spotlight: Best run when second of four in a Musselburgh novice (5f, soft) on final start for Iain Jardine last October; yet to hit top form for new yard (6f/5f), but has been dropped 10lb.
runners.comment: Best run on last start for Iain Jardine (5f); yet to hit form for new yard; on a good mark

runners.name: Tanaka
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on both runs for Kevin Ryan and no significant improvement after changing yards mid-season; back after a break but plenty to prove, particularly from outside draw.
runners.comment: Well beaten on all four starts last term and needs to improve markedly; poor draw

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mr Writer
runners.spotlight: Quite exposed; sound effort when runner-up off a mark of 77 in a Lingfield turf handicap last time (7f, good to firm); sets the standard.
runners.comment: Second off 77 in Lingfield turf handicap last time (7f, good to firm); sets the standard.

runners.name: Grasmere Boy
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Harry Angel; creditable second of ten in a 7f Newcastle novice on debut (33-1; two market leaders underperformed, but overall form fair); improvement likely; interesting on turf debut.
runners.comment: Shaped well when 2nd in AW novice on debut (33-1); better expected; interesting contender

runners.name: Two Commanders
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-brother to four winners at up to 1m2f; dam a 5f Listed winner; interesting newcomer who is worth a market check.
runners.comment: Half-brother to four winners at up to 1m2f; interesting newcomer; worth a market check

runners.name: Pacific Glory
runners.spotlight: Modest form so far (ninth of 11 in a C&D novice last time) and looks more one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Modest form (ninth of 11 in a C&D novice last time) and looks more one for handicaps.

runners.name: Brouhaha
runners.spotlight: Gelded and had a wind op before his debut in March when 17l sixth of 11 in a Southwell maiden (28-1; slowly away); that form working out quite well and better expected.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut (AW) but the form is working out quite well; better expected

runners.name: Gardening
runners.spotlight: 13-2 when 17l fifth of six in a Newcastle fillies novice when with Karl Burke last November (6f); bought for 1,000gns three weeks later; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Bought for 1,000gns after an inauspicious debut for Karl Burke last year; plenty to prove

runners.name: Captain Robert
runners.spotlight: Has been running creditably on the AW this year (in the frame on last three starts, all over 2m at Southwell); could go well, but below best three previous runs on turf.
runners.comment: Creditable run over 2m on AW this year; could go well but not at his best in 3 turf runs

runners.name: Tracker Issue
runners.spotlight: Ex-Jim Bolger; close second at Newcastle last November (1m4f) but poor efforts since, including back on turf after a break over 1m4f here last time; blinkers replace cheekpieces; tries this trip for the first time but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Close second on AW in November; poor form since (back on turf latest); blinkers now tried

runners.name: Lillistar
runners.spotlight: Over 6l behind Zephlyn over C&D last May; some fair form since (won over 2m at Wolverhampton in October) but not quite at her best back on turf last twice (over 2m5f and 1m4f); place chance.
runners.comment: Over 6l behind Zephlyn over C&D last May; hasn't been in top form but place chance

runners.name: Fleur De Mer
runners.spotlight: Not the best of win records (1-26 but 0-10 on turf); only win was over 1m2f on AW back in 2023; sound third at Pontefract with first-time cheekpieces (retained) on reappearance last month (1m4f, good to soft); stays 1m6f but below her best three previous tries at 2m.
runners.comment: 1-26; sound third over 1m4f on reappearance; below best three previous runs at around 2m
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing

runners.name: Not So Sobers
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/bumper winner (won 2m novice handicap hurdle on latest outing), but perhaps not as effective on the Flat, although was beaten only 1.75l when third of seven off a 2lb higher mark at Chelmsford in February (1m6f); a possible.
runners.comment: Hurdle/bumper winner but not as effective on the Flat, although fair 3rd on AW in February

runners.name: Zephlyn
runners.spotlight: In good form on the Flat this time last year, following up 2m win at Newcastle by winning twice over this C&D in May (good/good to firm); not been in top form on AW this year, but better run over hurdles last time; 5lb lower than last Flat win and is one with a chance.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner last May (good/good to firm); well treated now and is one with a chance

runners.name: Ramon Di Loria
runners.spotlight: Multiple AW and turf winner from 5f-7f; back on his last winning turf mark from Ayr 13 months ago (has twice won on AW since); below par latest but not ruled out if able to bounce back.
runners.comment: Back on his last turf winning mark; below par latest but not ruled out if bouncing back.

runners.name: Wen Moon
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on good or slower (placed here over 5f); inconsistent but put a lacklustre effort behind him when third at Carlisle (5f, good) latest; capable of being competitive.
runners.comment: Placed here (5f); third at Carlisle (5f, good) latest; capable of being competitive.
runners.owner: Mps Racing, D Shapiro & D Clifford

runners.name: Quercus
runners.spotlight: Loves it here, with all but one of his eight wins coming over C&D; not ground dependent; has been well below par in both starts since last winning (for Ann Duffield) here last September; not ruled out back here with 7lb claimed but it will need one of his better efforts from this mark.
runners.comment: Course specialist; below par last twice but not ruled out back here with 7lb claimed.

runners.name: Mark's Choice
runners.spotlight: Standing dish at Ripon, where he's recorded ten of his 12 wins; his 71st start is his first here; below par in both starts this season; drops 2lb but perhaps needs further favours from the handicapper to win again.
runners.comment: Ripon specialist; below par twice this season; needs further favours from the handicapper.

runners.name: Good Earth
runners.spotlight: Has been a superb servant, winning 16 of his 99 starts, including three this year; comfortable scorer at Haydock (6f, good to firm) latest, for which he has gone up 6lb; has won over C&D and looks a player again.
runners.comment: 16-99 career record, including over C&D; up 6lb for 2l win at Haydock latest; player.

runners.name: Holbache
runners.spotlight: Multiple sprint winner who runs here for the first time; 3lb higher than when last winning at Leicester (6f, good to soft) last September; not badly treated on that form but has yet to produce one of his better efforts following a break.
runners.comment: Not badly treated but has yet to produce one of his better efforts following a break.

runners.name: Evocative Spark
runners.spotlight: Arrives in great form, having won three of his four turf starts this season, two of those here over 7f and the latest over C&D; up another 4lb but looked like he had a bit more up his sleeve last time; big player.
runners.comment: In great form, winning three times here already this season; up 4lb; big player.

runners.name: Mademoiselle Belle
runners.spotlight: 5f AW winner who has been placed over 5f and 6f on turf; beaten just 1l from the same mark at Nottingham (6f, good) but well below that level when back there over 5f (good) latest; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 5f AW winner; 1l second on turf (6f, good) on penultimate start; below par (5f) latest.

runners.name: Populus
runners.spotlight: Showed minor ability in one of her qualifying starts (6f, AW) but was never involved on her handicap debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) latest; cheekpieces go on; more needed.
runners.comment: Never involved on handicap debut at Newcastle (7f) latest; cheekpieces go on; more needed.

runners.name: Mereside Spark
runners.spotlight: Best effort to date was at Beverley (5f, soft) last September but has been well below that level in two starts since (vet reported she bled from the nose latest); others preferred.
runners.comment: Best effort over 5f (soft); below par lately; bled from the nose latest; others preferred.

runners.name: Zoustar Dreams
runners.spotlight: 0-7 and a filly whose better efforts were both on AW surfaces; slipping to a workable mark and headgear again being tried (previously wore blinkers once but was last of five); need to see more.
runners.comment: 0-7 and better form on AW so far; workable mark and headgear back on but need to see more.

runners.name: Battenburg Belle
runners.spotlight: 0-7; better efforts have both been over 6f (good and good to soft), including at Ripon latest, when she raced prominently and stuck on well when the winner had asserted; up 2lb and looks a player.
runners.comment: Better efforts so far both over 6f; stuck on well when second at Ripon latest; player.

runners.name: Agric Ranger
runners.spotlight: The best of his three qualifying efforts (all last month) came on debut at Pontefract (6f, good to soft); initial mark looks a bit stiff based on what he's produced but no surprise if he improves now entering handicaps.
runners.comment: Initial mark stiff based on form but no surprise if he improves now entering handicaps.

runners.name: La Tosca
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability in three qualifying starts, all during this spring, on AW surfaces and at around 1m4f; looks to have been allotted a fair mark for her handicap debut and no surprise if she is a big player.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability; fair mark for handicap debut and no surprise if she is a player.
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Gemical
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in five starts over 7f-1m4f but did show some improvement over a similar trip at Thirsk (good) last time, when racing prominently; 2lb drop should help, while headgear is now tried; player.
runners.comment: 0-5 but showed some improvement latet (1m4f, good); drops 2lb; headgear tried; player.
runners.owner: G C Gale & Stuart Brown & Diane Stewart

runners.name: Favourite Friend
runners.spotlight: Has become a little more competitive since entering handicaps but still moderate form; cheekpieces swapped for blinkers; basement mark and will need some improvement to get involved.
runners.comment: Moderate form; blinkered; basement mark and will need some improvement to get involved.

runners.name: Red Rifle
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in six starts and has been well beaten in all three handicaps; slipping to a more workable mark but has plenty to prove at present, not least over this longer trip.
runners.comment: 0-6; workable mark but has plenty to prove at present, not least over this longer trip.

runners.name: Evergreen Emerald
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in four starts and beaten 14l on handicap debut at Ripon (1m2f, good) latest; drops 2lb; much more needed but longer trip should suit.
runners.comment: Beaten 14l on handicap debut at Ripon (1m2f, good); drops 2lb; longer trip a positive.

runners.name: Mohaasset
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in three qualifying starts and was 125-1 when an eased-down last of nine on her handicap debut at Redcar (1m, good to firm) latest.
runners.comment: Moderate form and was 125-1 when last of nine on handicap debut at Redcar (1m) latest.

runners.name: Star Of Emerald
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over 7f on his first two starts but slightly better over 1m latest (all three starts on Newcastle's Tapeta); gelded since last seen in February and, as a half-brother to two winners at around 1m4f, he may be capable of better now upped to this trip; trainer among the winners.
runners.comment: Moderate improvement latest and step up in trip should suit; stable among the winners.

runners.name: Emily Love
runners.spotlight: 5-9 over hurdles and earned a handicap mark of 129; wouldn't have won but was running well when coming down two out on chase debut at Killarney; much respected.
runners.comment: 129-rated hurdler; wouldn't have won but running well when falling on her chase debut

runners.name: Pampar Lady
runners.spotlight: Raised her game markedly last season over hurdles, winning three times to earn a mark of 118; trip/ground ideal for this first crack at chasing.
runners.comment: Raised her game last season over hurdles, winning three times and now rated 118

runners.name: Future Prospect
runners.spotlight: Can race freely but she was smart in bumpers and wasn't beaten far in a Listed hurdle at Punchestown last time (2m, yielding); 1-1 in points and brings significant potential to chase career.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in a Listed hurdle last time; 1-1 in points; good prospect for chasing

runners.name: Disco Dancer
runners.spotlight: One of two for the yard; bumper/dual winning hurdler; hasn't been at her best since returning from a year off and one or two others appeal more.
runners.comment: Bumper/dual winning hurdler; hasn't been at her best since returning from a year off

runners.name: Only A Dollar
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden over hurdles but not in the same league as her rivals in that discipline; limited appeal on chase debut.
runners.comment: Won a maiden over hurdles but not in the same league as her rivals in that discipline

runners.name: Beauforts Storm
runners.spotlight: Struggled in three runs over fences before another heavy defeat back over hurdles; she was smart in 2024 but now has much to prove.
runners.comment: Smart in 2024 but now has much to prove, including over fences

runners.name: Aclass
runners.spotlight: Won his beginners' chase here and ran right up to his best when beaten a neck over a tad shorter here last month; every chance off 3lb higher if in the same form.
runners.comment: Just missed out here last month and every chance off 3lb higher if in the same form

runners.name: Lake Chad
runners.spotlight: Veteran who enhanced his excellent record here with an 11l success over this trip last month, when the return to drier ground made a big difference; raised 8lb but he's historically well handicapped still.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner; raised 8lb but he's historically well handicapped still.

runners.name: Midnight Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner who is 0-10 over fences; has placed form but he fell in March and his jumping lacked fluency at Wexford last time.
runners.comment: He fell in March and his jumping lacked fluency at Wexford last time

runners.name: Dinoland
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest to predict but he bounded clear over nearly 3m at Downpatrick 22 days ago to earn this 7lb higher mark; good ground is the key to him.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but won well enough at Downpatrick to earn this 7lb higher mark
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & J Mackle & Mark Devlin & Philip Smith

runners.name: Miss Lia
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in a maiden hurdle here in 2024; below par in her previous chase and it's been all or nothing in her last four races over hurdles.
runners.comment: Below par in previous chase; all or nothing in her last four races over hurdles
runners.owner: Sussex By The Sea Syndicate/David L'Estrange

runners.name: Ferdia
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in seven races (mostly chasing) since returning from a mammoth absence.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in seven races (mostly chasing) since returning from a mammoth absence

runners.name: Killinure Lass
runners.spotlight: May have needed last month's run here after a break; runs this track well and won this 12 months ago off only 3lb lower; considered.
runners.comment: Runs this track well and won this 12 months ago off only 3lb lower

runners.name: Better Times
runners.spotlight: 5lb lower when winning a handicap hurdle in March; enjoys good ground and ran well for a long way on recent chase debut at Kilbeggan (2m3f, yielding); this trip likely suits him better and has possibilities in the new headgear.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler who ran well for a long way on recent chase debut at Kilbeggan

runners.name: Drop A Threat
runners.spotlight: Essentially disappointing given the impression he made in winning a point; beaten 17l and 46l in two chases, last time finishing behind Better Times; may do better in a handicap but not crying out to be backed.
runners.comment: Beaten 17l and 46l in two chases, last time finishing behind Better Times

runners.name: Jouster
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner for Gavin Cromwell before losing his way; some signs of a revival when fourth at Downpatrick (2m3f) and he's slipped to a handy mark.
runners.comment: Useful mark and some signs of a revival when fourth at Downpatrick

runners.name: The Rebel County
runners.spotlight: Hunter chase winner 12 months ago but fairly slim pickings since then and cheekpieces now go on.
runners.comment: Hunter chase winner 12 months ago but slim pickings since then and cheekpieces now go on

runners.name: Major Barry
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles in 2024 but lightly raced during the last 12 months and hasn't been anything like as good; potentially on a good mark for stable/chase debut but easy to have reservations.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner in 2024 but not a lot to show for exploits during past 12 months

runners.name: Ballybrack Wood
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m4f beginners' chase here in 2024; lightly raced since and was tailed off here last month following a long stretch on the sidelines; hard to enthuse over after that and cheekpieces are now fitted.
runners.comment: Lightly raced since his course win; tailed off here last month after a long absence

runners.name: Zolpharine
runners.spotlight: Hit with a 10lb rise for winning comfortably at Limerick (2m6f, good), which was a belated first win over fences; encouraging that he followed up over hurdles here last night, admittedly off a lower mark than today's; he's in the zone.
runners.comment: 10lb higher than at Limerick but he went in again over hurdles here last night

runners.name: The Border Boy
runners.spotlight: Only 1-21 over hurdles, the win coming on good ground at Wexford last summer (similar mark); did finish well held on chase debut in November and also in his subsequent run back hurdling.
runners.comment: Well held on chase debut in November and also in his subsequent run back hurdling

runners.name: My Good Pal
runners.spotlight: In first-time cheekpieces when a clear second over 2m7f at Downpatrick 22 days ago and the winner was Dinoland who contests the previous race; this 7yo gets in off the same mark.
runners.comment: Second 22 days ago and, although no match for the winner, he's on the same mark

runners.name: Menas Miracle
runners.spotlight: Two good runs in beginners' chases last October and could be all the sharper for her recent run over hurdles; this mark shouldn't be beyond her.
runners.comment: Two good runs in beginners' chases last October and possibilities off this sort of mark

runners.name: Mount Shenshan
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; has the chase form to feature off this figure but he was well beaten in a beginners' chase here only yesterday.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature off this mark but well beaten in a beginners' here yesterday

runners.name: Cullenwaine
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate and both wins were in 2024; however, chased home previous-race contender Lake Chad here before a near miss at Limerick last week; solid chance.
runners.comment: Chased home previous-race contender Lake Chad here before a near miss at Limerick

runners.name: Barely Made It
runners.spotlight: Well out of the weights last time so that's forgiven but was tailed off the run before; there's no substance to her chance.
runners.comment: Well out of the weights last time so that's forgiven but was tailed off the run before

runners.name: Mother Ship
runners.spotlight: Unable to win any of her 16 races over hurdles but did have some placed form and she's bred to make a chaser.
runners.comment: Had some placed form over hurdles and she's bred to make a chaser

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose gelding who won on the Flat last month; finished well for third over hurdles at Punchestown but over 2m and stamina could be an issue over this far on chase debut.
runners.comment: Won on the Flat last month; placed in 2m hurdle latest; stamina worries on chase debut

runners.name: Revolut Ned
runners.spotlight: Third in a hunter chase here last year and returns to rules action after winning a point two weeks ago; however, others have more pressing claims when this is his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Recent point winner; others have more pressing claims when this is his handicap debut

runners.name: Fr Gilligansvoyge
runners.spotlight: Rules record of 1-69 is a glaring negative but this veteran did run well to be fourth at Limerick nine days ago, finishing behind Cullenwaine.
runners.comment: 1-69 under rules but this veteran did run well to be fourth at Limerick nine days ago

runners.name: Le Roi Du Livet
runners.spotlight: Easily his best RPR was when a well-held third behind Shuttle Diplomacy at Naas in January; solid enough run when second in a Cork novice but he's now 0-8 and could be vulnerable once more.
runners.comment: Solid second in a Cork novice but he's now 0-8 and could be vulnerable once more

runners.name: Drombane
runners.spotlight: Best form is the 11l second in a maiden at Clonmel (2m3f, soft), after which he dropped away tamely here over 2m4f on yielding; Townend rides the yard's other one.
runners.comment: Dropped away tamely here in April and Townend rides the yard's other one

runners.name: Seven Towers
runners.spotlight: Placed in two points and not far away in his last two bumpers at Kilbeggan and Sligo (both on goodish ground); good pedigree and destined to improve over hurdles.
runners.comment: Placed in points/bumpers; good pedigree and destined to improve over hurdles

runners.name: Karma d'Airy
runners.spotlight: Bought for 250,000euros after finishing second in an Auteuil hurdle in 2024; given subsequent absence, presumably something was amiss when a remote third on stable debut in early 2025 (went off odds-on); interesting to gauge the level of confidence behind her.
runners.comment: Ability in France; remote third on yard debut in early 2025 when odds-on

runners.name: Western Shores
runners.spotlight: 15,000euros foal; dam unraced sister to winners Outlander (high-class bumper and 2m-3m hurdle/chase), Now McGinty (smart 2m5f hurdle/2m7f chase) and Western Leader (smart bumper and 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase); needs checking in the betting.
runners.comment: 15,000euros foal; related to some notable winners and needs checking in the betting

runners.name: Alaskan View
runners.spotlight: Managed to win only one of her eight bumpers and has been off since October; one to watch.
runners.comment: Managed to win only one of her eight bumpers and has been off since October; one to watch

runners.name: Teffian Warrior
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 64-rated on the Flat after seven defeats; has ability but wouldn't be the obvious answer on hurdle debut with stamina to prove; the tongue-tie is new.
runners.comment: Second reserve; wouldn't be the obvious answer on hurdle debut with stamina to prove

runners.name: Cocofred
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred and in top hands, but has yet to show much; remains best watched.
runners.comment: Nicely bred and in top hands, but has yet to show much; remains best watched

runners.name: Quest With Copper
runners.spotlight: First reserve; dam placed in 2m/2m2f hurdles, out of unraced half-sister to winners Mephisto (smart 1m4f-1m6f Flat and 2m2f-2m7f hurdle) and Almaydan (1m6f/1m7f Flat and useful 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase); one to watch.
runners.comment: First reserve; dam placed in 2m/2m2f hurdles; one to watch

runners.name: Princedici
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and on the retreat when departing late on at Punchestown.
runners.comment: 28-1 and on the retreat when departing late on at Punchestown

runners.name: Ripsnorter
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner for Andy Slattery last August but well beaten the following month on his hurdle/stable debut; has to concede weight to his principal rivals.
runners.comment: Bumper winner for a different yard; well held on hurdle/yard debut in September

runners.name: Victoria Kesia
runners.spotlight: 1m maiden winner for previous yard; nothing positive to report so far over hurdles.
runners.comment: 1m maiden winner for previous yard; nothing positive to report so far over hurdles

runners.name: Acheron
runners.spotlight: Some sign of ability on the Flat at up to 1m4f; 80-1 when tailed off in a maiden at Clonmel where his jumping lacked any fluency.
runners.comment: 80-1 when tailed off in a maiden at Clonmel where his jumping lacked any fluency

runners.name: China Grill
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; tailed off in two maidens on good to yielding ground.
runners.comment: Third reserve; tailed off in two maidens on good to yielding ground

runners.name: Woodie Campelltown
runners.spotlight: Placed in a point but tailed off in a bumper and two maiden hurdles.
runners.comment: Placed in a point but tailed off in a bumper and two maiden hurdles

runners.name: Secret Order
runners.spotlight: Weak form in points and triple-figure odds for his two maiden hurdles (tailed off and unseated midrace).
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds for his two maiden hurdles (tailed off and unseated midrace)

runners.name: Hastily
runners.spotlight: Has no form in maiden hurdles and she's now a low-grade Flat performer; no appeal.
runners.comment: Has no form in maiden hurdles and she's now a low-grade Flat performer; no appeal

runners.name: Slowdownbarney
runners.spotlight: With Gavin Cromwell when tailed off in his previous hurdle race in 2024; plenty of competitive Flat form since then for John McConnell but not overly appealing on this return to jumping action.
runners.comment: 8th in a hurdle in 2024; competitive Flat form subsequently for a different yard

races.race_name: Get A Free Month Trial Of Racing TV Now Racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Party Pack
runners.spotlight: 0-16 over hurdles; 5.5l second at Wexford last August off 2lb higher a standout effort; modest form both starts this year; others look more convincing.
runners.comment: Good run at Wexford last August, poor so far this year, others more convincing

runners.name: Bangor Lady
runners.spotlight: Failed to make an impact in four maiden hurdles; no sign of improvement on handicap debut at Sligo (held by Blue Moon Boy on that effort).
runners.comment: Poor maiden form, well held by Blue Moon Boy at Sligo latest

runners.name: Miss Donna
runners.spotlight: Showed a glimmer when fourth behind Cousin Kate in a maiden hurdle here in October and again when a 9l fifth of 13 in a handicap hurdle at Wexford on latest; eased 1lb for that; place chance if backing it up.
runners.comment: Best handicap effort to date on latest at Wexford, eased 1lb, place chance with repeat

runners.name: Liss Eile
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; best maiden hurdle effort came over C&D but beaten 16.5l at Clonmel (2m, soft) and pulled up here (16.5f, gd-yld) on latest in first two handicaps; eased 3lb but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Struggled in first two handicaps, pulled up over C&D latest, eased 3lb, others preferred

runners.name: Stormy Jenn
runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner; 0-14 over hurdles; okay run at Navan in November (2m, heavy) but pulled up when last seen at Fairyhouse in December; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-14 over hurdles, pulled up when last seen in December, others preferred

runners.name: Liceo
runners.spotlight: French Flat winner; 0-7 over hurdles but showed a bit of promise when fourth at Galway last September (16.5f, good); 4lb lower here and may have needed latest; yard in form.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles, 4lb lower than when fourth at Galway last year, yard in form

runners.name: Cool Summer
runners.spotlight: First reserve; handicap debutante; fine 3l third in Roscommon maiden on hurdles debut last August; didn't build on it but the type to improve now switched to handicaps; could go well if gets a run.
runners.comment: Promise on hurdles debut, could improve plenty for switch to handicaps; reserve

runners.name: Surviving Murmansk
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win came over C&D last August off 4lb lower; below best on Flat and over hurdles in three starts since; off since poor AW effort in November; likely to need this run.
runners.comment: C&D win last August, off since poor AW effort last Nov', may need this run

runners.name: Liberated Light
runners.spotlight: Back to form with 1.75l front-running second over C&D on penultimate off this mark; never travelled and didn't jump well when poor at Wexford last time; cheekpieces replace blinkers; could rebound back at this venue.
runners.comment: Good second here on penultimate, poor on latest, cheekpieces replace blinkers

runners.name: Las Brisas Boy
runners.spotlight: Best effort to date over hurdles a 7.25l fifth of 19 at Punchestown (2m, gd-yld) in first-time tongue tie (retained); needs more to get his head in front.
runners.comment: Best effort yet over hurdles on latest, tongue tie retained, needs more
runners.owner: Anthony Vaughan & Mark Phillips & D P Gaffney

runners.name: Esticky End
runners.spotlight: Flat winner is 0-13 over hurdles; didn't run terribly at Down Royal in December off this mark; should be sharper for latest run at Wexford when made late gains from the back; could outrun likely odds.
runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles, sharper for latest run at Wexford, could outrun likely odds

runners.name: Avandra
runners.spotlight: 0-17 and yet to trouble the judge; beaten 26l in first-time blinkers at Wexford on latest; blinkers now added; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-17, beaten 23l at Wexford latest, new headgear combo tried, hard to fancy

runners.name: Blue Moon Boy
runners.spotlight: 2-7 on the Flat; left maiden hurdle form behind when a keeping on 2.25l second of 15 on handicap debut at Sligo (2m2f, good); raised 3lb; debatable whether this sharper test will suit but he's a player.
runners.comment: Fine effort on handicap debut at Sligo (2m2f), up 3lb, shorter trip a query but a player

runners.name: Theanniversary Man
runners.spotlight: Has found form for this yard, notching up successive wins at Thurles (2m, gd-yld) and Cork (2m4f, sft-hvy) in November/December; has gone up a cumulative 14lb and has been off 174 days but can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Up 14lb for wins at Thurles and Cork, off 147 days, can't be discounted

runners.name: Topothelane
runners.spotlight: 9yo built on mildly promising third here in April (20.5f, yielding) to win easily at Wexford (2m1f, gd-yld) three days ago; 7lb penalty for that but should go well again if in the same form.
runners.comment: Off the mark by 11l at Wexford three days ago, 7lb penalty, should go well again

runners.name: Rogue Sea
runners.spotlight: Flat winner; no sign of winning potential in four maiden hurdles at 2m-2m1f but may improve for switch to a handicap; check market.
runners.comment: Flat winner, no show in maiden hurdles, could improve on h'cap debut, check market

runners.name: He Who Dares
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form so far on the Flat and in four runs over hurdles; hood now reached for; lacks a recent run; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Ordinary form on Flat and over hurdles, hood goes on, lacks recent run, hard to fancy

races.race_name: Group Packages And Discounts At Tramoreraces.ie Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Fabhcun
runners.spotlight: Rated 100; best effort over hurdles a 7l fifth of 7 on debut at Gowran (2m, heavy); beaten 12l at Kilbeggan on latest (15.5f, gd-yld), staying on late; looks sure to improve for this longer trip; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Stayed on late over shorter at Kilbeggan latest, longer trip a plus, cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Thiscouldbetheone
runners.spotlight: Promise on debut in Listed Limerick bumper (2m, soft); stayed on for a 25l sixth of 25 in much stronger maiden than this at Naas (2m, yielding) in January; very disappointing at Fairyhouse latest, however; cheekpieces and tongue tie go on for first start on good ground (brother City Island won three times on good ground).
runners.comment: Promise in strong Naas maiden hurdle, disappointing on latest, new headgear combo

runners.name: Nells Princess
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; promise in Punchestown bumper on Rules' debut; weakened after two out when upped to 22.5f on hurdles debut at Killarney on latest; more needed to take this.
runners.comment: Point winner, weakened over 22.5f on hurdles debut latest, more needed to win this

runners.name: Akako
runners.spotlight: First sign of ability on debut for this yard when keeping on for a never nearer 14l seventh of 17 at Clonmel (19.5f, gd-yld); this longer trip should suit.
runners.comment: Kept on for never nearer 14l seventh at Clonmel latest, step up in trip here a plus

runners.name: Tolka Row
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of three point-to-point starts; midfield in 2m3f maiden hurdles at Clonmel and Downpatrick (both gd-yld); will qualify for a handicap mark after this.
runners.comment: Placed in points, midfield in both maiden hurdles, qualifies for h'caps after this

runners.name: Butch Coolidge
runners.spotlight: From a good family but has yet to show winning potential in three runs over hurdles and rated 93; new hood needs to transform.
runners.comment: Rated 93 after three modest maiden hurdles runs, new hood needs to transform

runners.name: Headcorn
runners.spotlight: 2-15 in point-to-points; well beaten both starts under Rules in hunter chases at Limerick and Killarney; best watched on belated hurdles debut.
runners.comment: 2-15 on points, ordinary form in two hunter chases, watch on belated hurdles bow

runners.name: Rattledelatch
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on hurdles debut at Gowran in November; tailed off in a bumper at Punchestown latest; cheekpieces tried now.
runners.comment: Pulled up in maiden hurdle at Gowran, tailed off in bumper latest, cheekpieces tried
runners.owner: John Joseph Flynn & Conor Lannen & Mrs Miriam Queally

runners.name: Lady Cosmo
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner has been tailed off in both starts in maiden hurdles; best watched on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Point winner, tailed off in both maiden hurdles, new yard, best watched
runners.owner: Mrs Rose Boyd, Mrs Marie J. Armstrong, Seamus Carmichael

runners.name: Sharu
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a Down Royal bumper in December (2m, yielding) and a 2m Punchestown maiden hurdle (heavy) in January; off since and hopes pinned on good ground sparking some improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a bumper and on hurdles debut in January, off since, much more needed

runners.name: Majestic Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeats in a bumper and maiden hurdle, both at Kilbeggan; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats in a bumper and maiden hurdle at Kilbeggan, others preferred

runners.name: The Ducks Walk
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; huge odds for both starts over hurdles and ran accordingly; may do better when tackling handicaps.
runners.comment: Point winner, soundly beaten at huge odds in two maiden hurdles, one for handicaps

runners.name: Believe That
runners.spotlight: Veteran; 0-32 in point-to-points; 0-5 over hurdles and rated 92 but those five runs came back in 2021; not easy to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-32 in points, rated 92 over hurdles, not seen in this sphere since 2021, hard to fancy

runners.name: Installment
runners.spotlight: Very modest form so far in two bumpers and when tailed off in a Sligo maiden hurdle when last sighted 321 days ago; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Modest form in two bumpers and a Sligo maiden hurdles, seems safe to rule out

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (Mares) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Gortnaclea Castle
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two of her three bumpers, last time beating all bar a De Bromhead-trained favourite at Wexford (good to yielding); sets the standard.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two of her three bumpers and she sets the standard here
runners.owner: M O Quigley & Mrs H Lalor

runners.name: C'Est Elle
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point; beaten 9l and 15l in her two bumpers, latterly finishing behind Gortnaclea Castle; has to step up.
runners.comment: Beaten 9l and 15l in her two bumpers, latterly finishing behind Gortnaclea Castle

runners.name: Isabella Johnson
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in bumpers but it was a good run four days ago at Ballinrobe when third behind a promising winner (2m, good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-6 in bumpers but it was a good run four days ago when third behind a promising winner

runners.name: Arivabodiva
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Looksnowtlikebrian (useful 2m3f-3m2f hurdle/chase), Rivabodiva (2m7f/3m hurdle) and Divaboriva (bumper); dam 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase winner; immediate fitting of a hood is concerning but her yard has won the last two renewals of this bumper.
