runners.comment: Related to winners but the immediate fitting of a hood is concerning

runners.name: Cotswold Blue
runners.spotlight: 68,000euros yearling by Authorized; half-sister to winners Rickety Bridge (point/2m4f hurdle) and Raffles Capital (French 2m1f hurdle/2m2f chase); dam a half-sister to multiple Stayers' Hurdle winner Big Buck's; top yard and of obvious interest on debut, despite the immediate fitting of a hood.
runners.comment: Top yard and of obvious interest on debut, despite the immediate fitting of a hood

runners.name: Lavender Lady
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced in bumper/2m4f hurdle, out of 2m2f hurdle winning half-sister to high-class bumper and 1m7f-3m hurdle/chase winner Otago Trail; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: First foal; dam unplaced in bumper/2m4f hurdle; hooded for debut

runners.name: Gunner Girl
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to point winner All Night Parking; dam 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase winner; another newcomer in this race to be fitted with a hood from the outset.
runners.comment: Half-sister to point winner All Night Parking; dam 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase winner

runners.name: Bluebell Breeze
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced over hurdles/in points, half-sister to winners Crebilly (useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Lady Of The Sea (2m4f/3m hurdle); likely one for further down the line.
runners.comment: First foal; dam unplaced over hurdles/in points, half-sister to useful Crebilly

runners.name: Riverwoods
runners.spotlight: Her standout run is the second in a Wexford maiden hurdle last August; well held either side of that, so difficult to know what to expect.
runners.comment: Second in a Wexford maiden hurdle last August; well held either side of that
runners.owner: W N Cotter & R W Cotter

runners.name: Ronnie Returns
runners.spotlight: Completely tailed off in bumpers on heavy and soft ground; different conditions here if nothing else.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off in bumpers on heavy and soft ground

races.race_name: Julie Satterley Waving Goodbye To 49 Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Maskarvel
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in all three Irish bumpers last year and launched hurdling career with three very encouraging runs for new stable, most recently when clear second at Aintree (2m1f, good) 15 days ago; looks sure to run well.
runners.comment: Three encouraging runs over hurdles, most recently when second at Aintree 15 days ago.

runners.name: Machete Beach
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who scored twice on Flat over 1m2f in 2024 for Harry Charlton; returned from a 359-day break to open his account over hurdles in a weak Hereford maiden (2m, good) 25 days ago; has clearly had issues but possesses plenty of talent and should go close.
runners.comment: Opened his account over hurdles in a weak Hereford maiden 25 days ago.

runners.name: La Fille Friday
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m hurdle winner Aristo Du Plessis, out of maiden sister to French 2m1f chase winner Babati; best watched on this racecourse debut.
runners.comment: Best watched on this racecourse debut.

runners.name: Take The Boat
runners.spotlight: Four wins on AW Flat over 7f-1m4f at a modest level; interesting how she takes to this new discipline; may be one to fight out minor honours.
runners.comment: Four wins on AW Flat over 7f-1m4f at a modest level; hurdles debut.

runners.name: Ja Klipdrift
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros yearling; third foal; dam German 7f/1m winner, sister to useful 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Jan Maat, half-sister to 7f Listed winner Japan; probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Third foal from a German Flat winner; probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Usyk
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear promise when making the frame in a couple of bumpers last summer, but below that form when sixth of seven in a similar event at Worcester (2m, good; favourite) in September; makes hurdling debut and market will be best guide to his chance.
runners.comment: Showed clear promise in bumpers last year; hurdles debut.

runners.name: At Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Has shown just modest form in a couple of bumpers and two hurdle starts; easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Has shown just modest form in a couple of bumpers and two hurdle starts; easy to dismiss.

races.race_name: Happy 70th Birthday Rich Wilding Mares' Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Kottayam
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when placed a couple of times last year for Patrick Neville but pulled up in a couple of handicaps for Dylan Cunha and last of ten to complete on stable debut at Ludlow (2m5f, good) last month after six-month break; drops back in trip and tongue-tie is back on along with first-time cheekpieces; major revival required.
runners.comment: Placed in two maidens last year but has lost her way in three handicaps since.

runners.name: Stellarmasterpiece
runners.spotlight: Has won once on the Flat in Poland and four times at Catterick (1m4f), but the best she has been able to muster in seven runs over hurdles is when third of nine finishers at Doncaster (2m, good to soft) in November; interesting that Sean Bowen rides for the first time.
runners.comment: Has won five times on the Flat but best run over hurdles was when third at Doncaster.

runners.name: Liasse d'Argent
runners.spotlight: Has shown just modest form so far; sports first-time tongue-tie on handicap debut and improvement required.
runners.comment: Has shown just modest form so far; sports first-time tongue-tie on handicap debut.

runners.name: Little Lady Lucy
runners.spotlight: Relished the drop in trip and front-running tactics when getting off the mark in this sphere at Wincanton (1m7f, good; 25-1) last month; showed that was no fluke when following up at Taunton (2m, good) ten days later; 8lb higher here but this track should play to her strengths and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Got off the mark in this sphere at Wincanton last month; followed up at Taunton since.

runners.name: Blue Siam
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden and only minor promise in her three races over hurdles; she still has time on her side, though, and she swaps a hood for cheekpieces on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden and only minor promise in her three races over hurdles.

runners.name: Burgeon
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in four hurdle starts, including on handicap debut when pulled up at Warwick (2m5f, good) 26 days ago; sports first-time tongue-tie and cheekpieces, and they need to have an impact.
runners.comment: Has shown little in four hurdle starts, latest when pulled up at Warwick 26 days ago.

runners.name: For Love Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: Has shown only poor form in four runs over hurdles, including when fifth of six finishers at Taunton (2m, good) last month on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Has shown only poor form in four runs over hurdles.

runners.name: Noble Miss
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper for Rae Guest but has no form claims on what she's achieved over hurdles, including three handicaps at Fakenham; first-time blinkers are now applied along with hood.
runners.comment: Won a bumper for Rae Guest but has no form claims on what she's achieved over hurdles.

races.race_name: Julie Satterley 50th Birthday Celebration Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Juan Bermudez
runners.spotlight: All three wins over hurdles have been over C&D; returned to form when beaten a neck over C&D (good) last month and filled the same position at Aintree (2m1f, good) 15 days ago; is a major force around here and must come into the reckoning.
runners.comment: All three wins over hurdles have been over C&D; must come into the reckoning.

runners.name: Half A Chance
runners.spotlight: Irish challenger whose sole success came in a Limerick maiden on his hurdles debut in May 2024; placed five times last term and returns to this discipline after a couple of unsuccessful runs over fences this month; win record is offputting.
runners.comment: Irish challenger whose sole success came at Limerick on his hurdles debut in May 2024.

runners.name: In The Air
runners.spotlight: Won five over hurdles for previous yards and three over fences last term for current trainer; may have needed the run when seventh of eight finishers at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) last month after six-month layoff but probably needs to come down the weights.
runners.comment: Won five over hurdles, and three over fences last term for current trainer.

runners.name: Hope Rising
runners.spotlight: Won three in her first season over hurdles last term and may not have got home when third of five at Huntingdon (2m3f, good) on her return to action 23 days ago; should be sharper for the run and holds claims.
runners.comment: May not have got home when third at Huntingdon on her return to action 23 days ago.

runners.name: Forget The Way
runners.spotlight: Has three wins in this sphere and made a solid return when runner-up at Warwick (2m, good) ten days ago following a six-month break; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Has three wins in this sphere and was runner-up at Warwick ten days ago.

runners.name: Esperti
runners.spotlight: Has returned to form of late and gained his fifth chase success, at Ffos Las (3m, good) last month; there was no disgrace in finishing second to a resurgent Tommy Beau at Fakenham (3m5f, good) this month and he holds strong claims while in this form.
runners.comment: Has returned to form of late and gained his fifth chase success, at Ffos Las last month.

runners.name: Ballynaheer
runners.spotlight: Landed his fourth win over fences, at Market Rasen (3m, good) last month but below his best at Fontwell since; is the type to bounce back and he should get the trip (has won over 3m2f).
runners.comment: Landed his fourth win over fences, at Market Rasen last month.

runners.name: Yes Day
runners.spotlight: Both wins over fences came in March 2025; his season never took off last term and he arrives here on a career-low mark (11lb below last win); first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces; needs to show more.
runners.comment: Both wins over fences came in March 2025 but his season never took off last term.

runners.name: Imperial Alex
runners.spotlight: Four-times winning chaser over around 3m; returned to his best when beaten a neck at Ffos Las (3m, good) 25 days ago with 15l back to the third; nudged up 4lb but holds claims if he gets home over this marathon trip.
runners.comment: Back to his best when beaten a neck at Ffos Las 25 days ago with 15l back to the third.

runners.name: Ivane
runners.spotlight: Successful over fences (twice) and hurdles last term; went off the boil on his last two runs early on this year and is 7lb higher than his last winning mark; looks to need to come down the weights.
runners.comment: Successful over fences and hurdles last term but went off the boil on his last two runs.

races.race_name: Owen Thomas Memorial Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Delpotro
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose two wins last year came at Sedgefield when making all; his suspect stamina gave out in a match at Hereford (2m5f, good) 18 days ago but this track back down in trip should play to his strengths; regular hood returns after being omitted last time.
runners.comment: Front-runner whose two wins last year came at Sedgefield when making all.

runners.name: Rocks Up Late
runners.spotlight: Won four times over fences last spring/summer but was beaten 25l at Lingfield (2m, good) when last seen in November; has had wind surgery since and is only 1lb higher than for his last success; must hold claims from good yard if ready to roll.
runners.comment: Four wins last spring/summer but was beaten 25l at Lingfield when last seen in November.

runners.name: In This World
runners.spotlight: Won twice for the Skeltons; showed his best form for his current yard when fifth of eight at Warwick (2m, good) 26 days ago; his mark continues to drop and he should find another opening at some point.
runners.comment: Showed his best form for his current yard when fifth at Warwick 26 days ago.

runners.name: Bobbarelli
runners.spotlight: A dual winning pointer, he was runner-up twice over hurdles this year but disappointed when beaten 20l over fences at Bangor (2m4f, good) last Saturday; drops back in trip and may be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Disappointed when beaten 20l over fences at Bangor last Saturday.

runners.name: Turpin Gold
runners.spotlight: Four-times winning hurdler making a belated debut over fences after six-month break; hard to fancy unless there is a move in the market.
runners.comment: Four-times winning hurdler making a belated debut over fences after six-month break.

races.race_name: Happy 70th Birthday Steve Cotton Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Two To Tango
runners.spotlight: After being placed 12 times he gained an overdue success around here (2m3f, good) in March; let down by his jumping at Warwick since but looks sure to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: After being placed 12 times he gained an overdue success around here in March.

runners.name: Boom Boom
runners.spotlight: Won here over 2m1f (good) and an extended 2m3f (good to firm) last summer; subsequently lost his form in three runs; hard to gauge how he will run after a layoff but he would come into this if back on song.
runners.comment: Won here over 2m1f and an extended 2m3f last summer; subsequently lost his form.

runners.name: Gateau De Miel
runners.spotlight: His second success over fences came at this track (2m6f, good) last June; has been beaten in hunter chases/points since and makes stable debut; may be better suited by 3m but not out of this.
runners.comment: Won over fences here last June; has been beaten in hunter chases/points since.

runners.name: Lacrima
runners.spotlight: Won four times when trained in France last year but has been beaten 64l and 60l on his two starts in Britain; steps up in trip and blinkers worn last time are left off; hard to fancy until he shows a bit of form.
runners.comment: Won four times in France but has been beaten 64l and 60l on his two starts in Britain.

runners.name: Mix Of Clover
runners.spotlight: A summer jumper who mixes hurdling with chasing; won here over fences (2m5f, good) last October; returns from a six-month break and has never won following such an absence.
runners.comment: Won here over fences last October; returns from a six-month break.

races.race_name: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Shesonnet
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark up in trip at Warwick (2m5f, good; 33-1) 26 days ago on her handicap debut; lightly raced and there should be more to come from her.
runners.comment: Got off the mark up in trip at Warwick 26 days ago on her handicap debut.

runners.name: American Trio
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in points and showed little in first two starts for this yard in March; landed a gamble when dropped into a seller at Exeter (2m2f, good) last month in first-time visor (retained since); not disgraced when second of three at Huntingdon (2m4f, good to firm) on his handicap debut 11 days ago and should run well.
runners.comment: Won a seller at Exeter last month; second at Huntingdon on handicap debut recently.

runners.name: Lion Of The Desert
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; recorded back-to-back wins at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) recently; maintained his good level of form when third of five back there ten days ago; must come into this if he can replicate his form here.
runners.comment: Recorded back-to-back wins at Ffos Las recently; third there ten days ago.

runners.name: Masonbrook Meadow
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the 27th attempt under rules at Exeter (2m, good to firm) last October; ran her best since when runner-up at Fakenham (2m7f, good) 26 days ago; her versatility distance-wise holds her in good stead but her win-record is off-putting.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at the 27th attempt under rules at Exeter last October.

runners.name: Tao Enki
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but ran his best race to date when runner-up at Hereford (2m, good) 25 days ago in first-time cheekpieces (retained); steps up in trip but knocking on the door and should go close.
runners.comment: 0-7 but ran his best race to date when runner-up at Hereford 25 days ago.

runners.name: Shes All Pride
runners.spotlight: Showed first form over hurdles when third of nine over C&D (good) in March on her handicap debut; failed to replicate that effort at Ludlow since.
runners.comment: Showed first form over hurdles when third over C&D in March on her handicap debut.

runners.name: Parikarma
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over hurdles but she has been below her best since returning from a 436-day break in February; down to a career-low mark in this sphere but best watched for now.
runners.comment: Has been below her best since returning from a 436-day break in February.

runners.name: Miss Tiara
runners.spotlight: Had three runs since a wind operation; signs in her last two races that she's found her level, beaten under 7l in 3m handicaps at Taunton; this trip might be a better fit; considered.
runners.comment: Yet to get her head in front but signs in her last two races that she's found her level.

runners.name: Great d'Ange
runners.spotlight: Has four wins over fences but yet to score in this sphere; has shown nothing in his three runs for current yard.
runners.comment: Has shown nothing in his three runs for current yard.

runners.name: Roxboro River
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles when winning over C&D (good to firm) last July off 1lb lower; hasn't been placed since but dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over hurdles when winning over C&D last July off 1lb lower.

runners.name: Keep It Classic
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after six starts but lost out only by a short head over similar trip to today's at Haydock (good; wore cheekpieces) three weeks ago, despite racing too freely; might be more relaxed with the headgear left off here; respected.
runners.comment: Went close at Haydock this month and might settle better with headgear left off here

runners.name: Lucky Sevens
runners.spotlight: Opened account when fitted with cheekpieces for turf debut at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) last month and was placed over 1m2f on both starts since, more recently when clear second to much-improved rival at Bath (firm); upped in trip again here; leading contender.
runners.comment: Placed twice over 1m2f since last month's 1m win; upped in trip again here; big player

runners.name: Mick The Hat
runners.spotlight: Beaten at least 12l in his three qualifying races (1m-8.6f), showing nothing more than glimmers of promise, but moves up in trip here and ought to be much more competitive in this low-grade handicap.
runners.comment: Safely held in all three qualifying races but should be more competitive on handicap debut

runners.name: Timely Salute
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in both handicaps, more recently when last of nine at Bath (1m2f, firm; Lucky Sevens finished second) this month; bred to be suited by this longer trip but needs significant improvement.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both handicaps; significant improvement needed over new trip here

runners.name: Song Of The Stars
runners.spotlight: Safely held when third in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) three weeks ago and now 0-6; needs to find something extra on this turf debut.
runners.comment: Safely held when third on AW this month and now 0-6; more needed on turf debut

runners.name: Ada Rose
runners.spotlight: Evidently suited by big hike up in trip (from 7f) when third of 11 on last month's handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW); that run came after a break and she could easily have more to offer if handling switch to turf.
runners.comment: Improved form when placed on last month's handicap debut (1m4f, AW); turf debut today

runners.name: Panthere Noir
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; bettered low-key stable debut and belied 50-1 odds when third of 14 at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm; refitted cheekpieces) one week ago; still unexposed over this sort of trip; a possible.
runners.comment: Belied 50-1 odds when third at Windsor last week and remains unexposed over this trip

runners.name: Naranka
runners.spotlight: Always behind when 50-1 for seasonal debut at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm; Panthere Noir third) one week ago; needs to have taken considerable benefit from that outing.
runners.comment: Always behind when 50-1 for seasonal debut a week ago

runners.name: Out In The Cold
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a telling blow off basement marks in her first three handicaps (1m-1m2f); moves up in trip and tries cheekpieces here.
runners.comment: Unable to land a telling blow off basement marks in her first three handicaps

runners.name: Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Raced rather lazily and finished about 6l behind Lucky Sevens when fourth on last month's seasonal/handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) but has obvious scope to build on that performance over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Just a respectable fourth on seasonal/handicap debut (1m2f) but has scope to build on that

runners.name: Mister Pretentious
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise when a 250-1 outsider for his three qualifying races (1m-1m2f) and makes handicap debut from out of the weights; upped in trip with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Showed no significant promise when a 250-1 outsider for his three qualifying races

runners.name: Head Girl
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four runs but was unable to justify prominence in betting when down the field on last month's seasonal/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW); needs a good boost from the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Unexposed after only four runs but finished down the field on seasonal/handicap debut

races.race_name: Drain Detectives Premier Drainage Company Handicap

runners.name: Cezarro
runners.spotlight: Regressive, ex-Irish maiden who's been expensive to follow on the AW this spring; below par back in headgear (left off tonight) on his return to turf ten days ago.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow on the AW this spring; below par back on turf ten days ago

runners.name: Pride Of Nepal
runners.spotlight: Frequent slow starter who was the biggest price of his trainer's three runners when winning easily at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) last Saturday, without the usual tongue-tie/cheekpieces, as today; had looked on the downgrade on the AW but happier on turf; leading claims back up 6lb.
runners.comment: Happier on turf; leading claims back up 6lb for last weekend's easy Windsor win

runners.name: Haveagobeau
runners.spotlight: Dual Polytrack winner here this year, latterly in 0-50 company; didn't look to have any excuses back on turf (now 0-19) off this mark early in the month and needs more.
runners.comment: Didn't look to have any excuses back on turf (now 0-19) off this mark early in the month

runners.name: Crafter
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 and is having his first run on turf in three years; down to a career-low mark and has been in fair form in blinkers, but he's regressive and has returned three poor efforts in turf handicaps.
runners.comment: In fair form on AW but regressive and is having his first turf run in three years

runners.name: Twilight Guest
runners.spotlight: Landed hat-trick this time last year, including two round Brighton, so this track won't be a concern; a string of poor efforts since, though, including when beaten a long way behind Pride Of Nepal at Windsor last week.
runners.comment: Notched a hat-trick this time last year; string of poor efforts since though

runners.name: Duke Orsino
runners.spotlight: One of his better efforts at Yarmouth one month ago, when going beyond 1m for the first time, but was probably flattered in a muddling, small-field affair; will want to settle better upped in distance again.
runners.comment: One of his better efforts last time but was probably flattered; up in trip again

runners.name: Shifra
runners.spotlight: Poor under both codes, again starting a big price back on the Flat five weeks ago; hard to make a case for her, even in such a weak handicap.
runners.comment: Poor under both codes; hard to make a case for her, even in such a weak handicap

runners.name: Stoneacre
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden who's never gone close to finishing placed (7f-1m6f); the switch from cheekpieces to blinkers didn't make any difference back on turf one month ago.
runners.comment: Poor maiden who's never gone close to finishing placed (7f-1m6f); blinkers no help latest

races.race_name: Drain Detectives CCTV Drain Surveys Handicap

runners.name: Footwork
runners.spotlight: Progressed during a light campaign last season and turned in a career-best effort over this trip for the first time at Ayr (good to firm) ten days ago; holds decent claims upped 3lb under 3lb claimer Jack Callan, who's enjoying a fine season and is 10-44 for the yard.
runners.comment: Holds decent claims under 3lb claimer Jack Callan (10-44 for yard) upped 3lb for Ayr win

runners.name: Quamby
runners.spotlight: Maiden who missed a lot of last year but has returned a string of solid efforts in defeat, including in handicap company the past twice; inclined to over-race on her comeback from five months off and is entitled to improve; considered.
runners.comment: Inclined to over-race on comeback from five months off and entitled to improve; considered

runners.name: Epictetus
runners.spotlight: Things didn't pan out his way on first crack at 1m4f here last time (AW) and that was another in-form effort; has found his level again but wants a good test over this trip, he hasn't won in a long time and has become inconsistent on turf.
runners.comment: Found his level again but wants a good test over this trip and hasn't won in a long time

runners.name: Post Rider
runners.spotlight: Her wins after breaks of three months and four months are a positive as she returns from a layoff; resumes on a career-high mark, though, having closed out last year with a second on Tapeta, and she'll need to be spot on to win this; may be the one to lead.
runners.comment: Resumes on a career-high mark and she'll need to be spot on; may be the one to lead

runners.name: Hitched
runners.spotlight: A regular on the AW here (three wins) who's much higher in the weights than when last seen frequently on turf; generally consistent but whether he's up to this mark on grass is the leading question, and Quamby looks his trainer's best chance.
runners.comment: Much higher in the weights than when last seen frequently on turf; tough task

runners.name: Red Hat Eagle
runners.spotlight: Has not run badly in two starts back from around 18 months off but has never been quite this far before; goes in first-time cheekpieces (worn a hood twice before) back from ten weeks off.
runners.comment: Not run badly in two starts back from around 18 months off; goes in first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: Sweet Reward
runners.spotlight: Generally kept away from fast ground but won easily on good to firm over 1m2f here for Olivia Tubb early in the month; retains most of her ability for a yard enjoying a good spring but she's often come to hand early in the past and a 7lb rise will test her.
runners.comment: Yard enjoying a good spring; 7lb rise for her recent win over 1m2f will test her

races.race_name: APC Electrical EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Matriarchal
runners.spotlight: On the go early and put her experience to good use when making all on rain-softened ground at Navan a fortnight ago; needs more under a penalty (experienced Harry Vigors taking off 7lb will help) as she becomes her trainer's first runner outside Ireland.
runners.comment: Needs more under a penalty as she becomes her trainer's first runner outside Ireland

runners.name: Queencard
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling who's out of Norfolk Stakes winner Shang Shang Shang; showed plenty of pace before cracking late over Newmarket's 6f on debut and her claims are obvious dropping back in trip from a handy draw.
runners.comment: Plenty of pace before cracking late over Newmarket's 6f on debut; her claims are obvious

runners.name: Alta Regina
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns yearling, 240,000gns breeze-up 2yo who's bred to be sharp, being out of a 2yo 5f winner and a half-sister to two 2yo winners, one on debut; trainer 7-23 with juveniles on turf (this is his first 2yo runner of the season) and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 240,000gns breeze-up 2yo who's bred to be sharp; the market will assist

runners.name: Glamorize
runners.spotlight: Late-foaled £48,000 yearling, £155,000 breeze-up 2yo; yard among the winners and is an excellent 4-12 with 2yos this season (two winning on debut); in the same ownership as George Boughey's pair and the market will doubtless tell a tale.
runners.comment: Late-foaled £48,000 yearling, £155,000 breeze-up 2yo; yard 4-12 with 2yos this season

runners.name: Blue Sign
runners.spotlight: April-foaled 32,000gns yearling, £50,000 breeze-up 2yo who's a half-sister to two 1m AW winners; trainer, who's had a big-priced 2yo win on debut this spring, has a better record with juveniles on the AW and she may want more of a test in due course.
runners.comment: April-foaled 32,000gns yearling, £50,000 breeze-up 2yo; may want more of a test

runners.name: Roxa Love
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to Natural Force, who won on 2yo debut, and Mademoiselle, plus three other lesser lights; well drawn on debut (next to stablemate Queencard).
runners.comment: 100,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to Natural Force, who won on 2yo debut; handy draw

runners.name: Targa
runners.spotlight: £105,000 breeze-up 2yo whose trainer has enjoyed a good spring with juveniles; it was game over at the stalls on her Goodwood debut last weekend (very slowly away), so that can be ignored, but she was easy to back and it's possible she'll repeat the dose.
runners.comment: Very slowly away on debut last weekend, so that can be ignored, but she was easy to back

runners.name: Charcoal Diva
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling who started a three-figure price when beaten a long way on her Yarmouth debut recently; needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: Started a three-figure price when beaten a long way on her Yarmouth debut recently
runners.owner: B Keane, S Nugent & G4 Contracts

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C & D) (Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Sword Salute
runners.spotlight: Out of a very useful 7f/1m winner and made promising debut when third of seven at Redcar (7f, good; winner could be smart) 12 days ago; already sets a fairly good standard and improvement is likely.
runners.comment: Promising debut when third at Redcar 12 days ago (winner could be smart)
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Nakila Sun
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; fourth foal; dam unraced sister to German 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Navarra King, out of an Italian 1m2f Group 3 winner; in good hands; market may guide.
runners.comment: Out of unraced sister to German 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; in good hands; market may guide

runners.name: Wadima
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 9.4f AW winner (RPR 71), half-sister to 7f winner Zabeir, out of unraced half-sister to Listed winners Zanughan (1m4f) and Zand (10.5f); worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: First foal from a fair 9.4f AW winner; worth a glance in the betting

runners.name: Gliding Martha
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to 1m1f winner Loveable Rogue (RPR 72); dam runner-up 5f-1m (75), half-sister to Group 3 winners Burnt Sugar (6f 2yo) and Brown Sugar (5f/6f 2yo); would be a rare winning newcomer for the stable.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to two Group 3 sprint winners; would be rare winning newcomer for yard

runners.name: Solar Maximus
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Brassica (Listed-placed 7.4f 2yo; RPR 96) and Von Baer (7f; 91); dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (88), half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Dream Of Dreams, family of top sprinter Airwave; looks the part on paper.
runners.comment: Out of a Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner from a top sprinting family; appeals on paper

runners.name: Able Tasman
runners.spotlight: 46,000euros yearling; tenth foal; closely related to 9.4f-2m winner Antiquarium (RPR 112), half-brother to three winners including Grand Scheme (1m-1m6f; 92); dam 1m4f AW winner (76), half-sister to smart 1m2f-1m6f winner Scott's View; will probably need longer trips to be seen to best effect but first-time-out 2yos from this stable are usually of interest.
runners.comment: Bred for longer trips than this but stable's first-time-out 2yos are usually of interest

runners.name: Pj's Corner
runners.spotlight: £30,000 yearling; second foal; half-brother to 5f AW winner Houndhill (RPR 72); dam unraced sister to Listed-placed 5f winner Lokada; stable has only occasional first-time-out 2yo winners.
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to a Listed-placed 5f winner; stable not prolific with newcomers

runners.name: Ariane Sky
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear promise on both starts, more recently when keeping-on fifth at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) nine days ago, but needs improvement for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Displayed clear promise on both starts (5.7f/6f) but needs improvement for this new trip

runners.name: Sakowin
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and showed only minor promise before weakening into eighth at Newbury (6f, good) a fortnight ago; this race looks easier, though, and he ought to have learnt from that experience.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise when eighth at Newbury (6f) but this race looks easier

runners.name: Vidmiyr
runners.spotlight: Denied clear run when placed off today's mark on this month's handicap debut at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) and did not need to improve to make all at skinny odds in 7f course maiden (good) 11 days ago; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark on handicap debut and has since made all in a course maiden
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Raging Raj
runners.spotlight: Won two AW nurseries in November (1m/7f) and began this campaign with two respectable runs in competitive 1m turf handicaps; the most exposed runner in the line-up but sure to appreciate this ease in grade.
runners.comment: The most exposed runner in the line-up but sure to appreciate this ease in grade

runners.name: Velvet Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to prevail by a head against her own sex at Catterick (7f, good) nine days ago and now 2-2 in handicaps; another 3lb higher over a slightly longer trip but is probably still improving; has big role to play.
runners.comment: Narrow winner at Catterick (7f) nine days ago and now 2-2 in handicaps; respected
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Get Outta Here
runners.spotlight: Appeared to display improvement when third of nine behind 1-12 favourite at Epsom (8.5f, good) last month, on third start; that form is quite tricky to weigh up but the running-on fourth has done well in the meantime; drops back in trip for handicap debut; remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Placed behind 1-12 winner in 8.5f Epsom novice last month; back in trip for handicap debut

runners.name: Berkshire Boom
runners.spotlight: Absent 289 days after winning Chester maiden (7f, good) last summer and made no more than a respectable seasonal/handicap debut when midfield at Windsor (1m, good to firm) this month; needs that recent outing to have brought him on.
runners.comment: 7f 2yo maiden winner; made just a satisfactory seasonal/handicap debut this month
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Accademia
runners.spotlight: Posted most encouraging of three qualifying runs when keeping-on fourth at Kempton (7f, AW) in October; has fitness to prove after seven-month absence but should have more improvement to come in handicaps at some stage this season.
runners.comment: Absent since fourth in AW novice in October but open to improvement in handicaps this year

races.race_name: Drain Detectives No.1 For Drains Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Woolridge
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who ran his best race for this yard, back up to 6f and under more aggressive tactics, on Newcastle Tapeta last time; now needs to build on it on just his third run on turf.
runners.comment: Best effort for this yard when back at 6f on Tapeta last time; now needs to build on it

runners.name: Mission Command
runners.spotlight: Has been running over further of late, looking to have no problem with 1m last month; pulled hard when below par last time and there are risks involved now first-time visored (has worn cheekpieces twice) dropped back to sprinting.
runners.comment: Running over further of late; risks involved in a first-time visor dropped back to 6f

runners.name: Twilight Jet
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 7yo who had a much higher mark this time last year; well backed all day and on course when winning over C&D 12 days ago, and there's every chance he can defy a 3lb rise under Ashley Lewis, who has plenty of experience for his 3lb claim in this company.
runners.comment: Well backed when winning over C&D 12 days ago; there's every chance he can defy a 3lb rise

runners.name: Invincible Speed
runners.spotlight: Consistent gelding who's usually ridden from off the pace; no obvious reason why he won't go well once more, back over an easier 6f, returning from a short break.
runners.comment: No obvious reason why he won't go well, back over an easier 6f, after a short break

runners.name: The Bitter Moose
runners.spotlight: Has done his winning, and a lot of his racing, over 7f; didn't get the breaks back down to 6f last time following a fair AW comeback, and he's capable of going well off this mark.
runners.comment: Didn't get the breaks back down to 6f last time; capable of going well off this mark

runners.name: Lahina Bay
runners.spotlight: Most wins have come in the second half of the year and always after a recent outing; returns from four months off and she might need a bit more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Likely to come on for the run and she might need a bit more help from the handicapper

runners.name: Providence
runners.spotlight: Terrific in the Frank Packer Plate Randwick two back. Fought bravely to the wire in the Rough Habit Plate at Doomben. Should appreciate stepping up in distance. Fit from racing and right in the mix again.
runners.owner: Newgate Bloodstock, China Horse Club Racing Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: One Step Closer
runners.spotlight: Very tame performance in rain affected going at Doomben. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: C E Holt, R J Miller Et Al

runners.name: Southern Prince
runners.spotlight: Upped in distance when reasonable effort without seriously threatening in rain affected going at Doomben. Unexposed at trip. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: Vieira Racing, B Vieira Et Al

runners.name: O'Sheamus
runners.spotlight: Steps out for third run new stable/region. Well back and never a factor in choppy conditions at Doomben. New to Eagle Farm. Very hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: N J Hoar, Mrs I O'Shea Et Al

runners.name: Amazake
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Caulfield Galilee Series Final placegetter 11 Apr. Started $11 when battled home in Open company in rain affected going at Morphettville. Usually gets back.
runners.owner: Ciaron Maher Racing, C M R Diminish Et Al

runners.name: Kilman
runners.spotlight: Sent forward from the wide gate and slipped clear in choppy conditions to a decisive victory at Doomben. Has built in speed. Advantaged if soft. Should relish the rise in distance.
runners.owner: N Morgan & D H T Devine

runners.name: Options
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in the slosh at Ipswich. Strikes a harder race.

runners.name: Beauty Swift
runners.spotlight: Took out the (Bm64) at Kembla Grange 29 Apr. Handy, knuckled down with purpose in rain affected going at Doomben. First try at 2400m. Hard to warm to.

runners.name: Different Gravy
runners.spotlight: Won the Melbourne Classic Caulfield 29 Nov. Started $7 when safely held in Open company at Flemington. Has his first look at 2400m here. One for multiples.

runners.name: Solid Gold
runners.spotlight: Won Championship Stakes Ellerslie 18 Apr. Okay at latest when settled back. Likes the sting out. Has her first look at 2400m here.

runners.name: Accidental Bid
runners.spotlight: Bolted in at Pakenham two back. Honest performance over 2000m at Doomben. Should relish the rise in distance. Profiles well.
runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock, G & C Pastoral Co Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Defendant
runners.spotlight: Randwick-Kensington 3yo+ (Bm72) placegetter 27 Apr. Sent out $29 when battled home to finish 8th in rain affected going at Doomben. Rise to 2400m a new challenge. Others carry more punch.
runners.owner: C J Waller, Marcstown Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Michelangelo
runners.spotlight: Third run now in new stable/region. Finished too well winning (Bm64) at Hawkesbury. Rise to 2400m a new challenge. Stiffer challenge here.
runners.owner: Mrs G L Muollo, Miss N A Muollo Et Al

runners.name: Inspired Legend
runners.spotlight: Went home tamely in rain affected going at Doomben at $51. Rise to 2400m a new challenge. Looking elsewhere.

runners.name: Certify
runners.spotlight: Didn't quicken with the leading division but stayed on okay late at Doomben. Rise to 2400m a new challenge. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: Magic Bloodstock Racing, Sunlight Syndicate Et Al

runners.name: Castello Grande
runners.spotlight: Annexed the 3yo Open Hcp at Eagle Farm 6 May. Went home tamely in choppy conditions at Doomben at $60. First try at 2400m. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: Mrs V A Heathcote, M E Kleimeyer Et Al

runners.name: Valiant Sun
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. Settled midfield, lacked required acceleration at Flemington over a longer assignment. Keener on others.
runners.owner: Liam Howley Racing Pty Ltd, Wentwood Grange Et Al

runners.name: Monopolistic
runners.spotlight: Off midfield when flew home to score at Gosford. Has his first look at 2400m here. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: Newgate Bloodstock, China Horse Club Racing P/L Et Al

runners.name: Falcon Lair
runners.spotlight: Placed 3yo Hcp at Doomben 11 Apr. Sent out $23 when unable to threaten in rain affected going at Doomben. First try at 2400m. Others with more appeal.
runners.owner: Spartus Bloodstock, P L Ward Et Al

runners.name: Chillies
runners.spotlight: Went home tamely in the slop at Ipswich at $5.50. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: Grahame Begg Racing, R W Gamble Et Al

runners.name: Contingency
runners.spotlight: Ran on okay without troubling in the slop at Ipswich. Has his first look at 2400m here. Hard to make a case.

runners.name: Eynesbury
runners.spotlight: On a short back-up. Not disgraced in rain affected going at Randwick. Stamina on trial here. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: A Kheir, Yes Bloodstock Et Al

runners.name: Buthelezi
runners.spotlight: Third run now in new stable/region. Courageous effort in the 3yo Hcp at Flemington at second run back. First try at 2400m. One for exotics.
runners.owner: T J Delroy & Mrs T D Delroy

runners.name: Jimmysstar
runners.spotlight: Outstanding gelding. Terrific in the All Aged Stakes Randwick two back. Gave solid chase in a good effort at Doomben when firmed from $2.90 into $2.25. Placed only other run at Eagle Farm.
runners.owner: A Kheir, J A O'Neill Et Al

runners.name: Headley Grange
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Won the 3yo+ Qlty Rosehill 11 Oct. Handy and acquitted himself fairly well at Randwick. Since trialled at Warwick Farm 25 May.
runners.owner: R J Manwaring, S C Foley Et Al

runners.name: Private Eye
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Veteran warrior and big race specialist. Off pace, hit line forcefully at Randwick first up. Drawn to effect.
runners.owner: Proven Thoroughbreds, P Yates Et Al

runners.name: Skybird
runners.spotlight: Ran home respectably into a non-threatening 7th at Doomben. New to Eagle Farm. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: M J Scalzo, D J Scalzo Et Al

runners.name: Linebacker
runners.spotlight: Ridden in back half, strong late to fill the minors Luskin Star Stakes at Scone at this trip. First time around Eagle Farm. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: F W Cook, Mrs C M Cook Et Al

runners.name: Splash Back
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Gave them a start but picked up strongly to win quite impressively in Victory Stakes this venue in second run from spell. Usually gets back. Hard to fancy, despite latest trial win.
runners.owner: Ronball, C McGregor & Miss Z J McGregor

runners.name: Rothfire
runners.spotlight: Seasoned big-race specialist. Won in decisive fashion at Doomben in second run from spell. One for exotics.
runners.owner: Mrs V A Heathcote, Miss S R Freeman Et Al

runners.name: Sixties
runners.spotlight: Let-up (56-days). Won the Phar Lap Stakes at Rosehill two back 14 Mar. Modest showing (wide draw) in rain affected going at Randwick. Has an adverse draw and rising in weight.

runners.name: Transatlantic
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Terrific in the Five Diamonds Rosehill two back. Settled close and ran an honest race in rain affected ground here. Will be fitter second-up.
runners.owner: Mrs Z A Daly, Segenhoe Thoroughbreds Australia Et Al

runners.name: Napoleonic
runners.spotlight: Fought bravely to the wire in the Doomben 10,000 at Doomben at first run for new trainer. Can fill the minors here.

runners.name: Grafterburners
runners.spotlight: Won the M.M Sunlight Plate Gold Coast 10 Jan. On pace but folded up quickly at Doomben in second run from spell. Home track advantage but awkwardly drawn. Stripping fitter and shapes as a rough place prospect.
runners.owner: G A Betts, K S Swindail Et Al

runners.name: King Of Roseau
runners.spotlight: Has a picket fence form line. Got up in final strides at Gosford. A distance specialist. Meets stronger company here but in good order.
runners.owner: C T Hay, P M O'Shea Et Al

runners.name: Wodeton
runners.spotlight: Let-up (70-days). Gave good account on a soft deck Darby Munro Stakes at Rosehill. Since trialled at Randwick 15 May. First time around Eagle Farm.

runners.name: Desert Lightning
runners.spotlight: Responded well from back half to get the prize at Morphettville in second run from spell. Sharper now and goes into wider exotics.

runners.name: Another Wil
runners.spotlight: Battled home in rain affected going at Doomben when off an extended spell. First time around Eagle Farm. Keener on others.
runners.owner: Halo Racing Services Pty Ltd, Mrs Janice McKenna Et Al

runners.name: Sepals
runners.spotlight: Let-up (63-days). Finished well held in soft going at Rosehill at second run back. Has a tricky draw. New to Eagle Farm.
runners.owner: C I Brown, H A Brown Et Al

runners.name: Fangirl
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Won the 3yo+ WFA Randwick 20 Sep. Ran on late from the back at Randwick when off an extended spell. Since trialled at Rosehill 11 May.

runners.name: Von Hauke
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Ridden cold and let down powerfully at Bendigo second-up. Usually gets back. Drops to shorter trip.
runners.owner: Barree Pastoral Co Pty Ltd & Imperial Racing Stable

runners.name: Zou Sensation
runners.spotlight: Last assignment this trip when handily placed and worked home soundly at Scone. Will be fitter second-up. First time around Eagle Farm. Looks up against it.
runners.owner: Bennett Racing Pty Ltd (N Bennett), D Hardie Et Al

runners.name: Yellow Brick
runners.spotlight: Strong Open 1300 win from midfield at Rockhampton first up. Has a tricky draw and up in weight. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: Mrs L J Sears, Ms M L Sears & Mrs S E Wee
